text:
Robotics has been spoke about a lot this weekend. Certainly feels like the algo is pushing it in my direction.

Initially when $OUST was getting pushed in my direction months ago, it appeared to be one of the many tickers that circulates X due to group speak.

The fact it’s circulating my feed again now though.. months later, intrigues me.

Look, physical AI is the next frontier of the AI buildout. It will be the next wave.

Do I think it is incredibly early? Yes.

But early often is where the highest level of alpha is found.

Once I’ve completed researching into photonics / opticals/ connectivity I will be delving into the perspective of physical AI.

Before I start diving into individual companies I need to understand thoroughly the entire sector and when the sector is likely to move as a whole.

Do you own any stocks currently within the robotics trade? $OUST so far is the main ticker I see circulating X and of course $TSLA but this is more so product not the component required to put together the product.

text:
$SIVE  found a strategic edge hidden within O-NET : 3SP Technologies, a French specialty foundry in Nozay that now locks down the photonic AI supply chain.

This is not just a commercial partnership; it is a full-stack vertical integration strategy. O-NET serves as the industrializing force that integrates Sivers' high-performance laser IP into one of the most robust photonics solutions on the market. 

By plugging directly into this end-to-end industrial chain, Sivers is positioned in the optimal place to capture the surging demand for AI infrastructure, having solved the industry's hardest challenge: scaling photonics from the lab to the data center.

The winning equation for next-generation architecture:
$SIVE  (Design): High-performance DFB laser array architecture.

3SP Technologies (French Fab): Specialty foundry mastering III-V material epitaxy (InP).

O-NET (Scale): High-volume industrial assembly of ELS (External Light Source) modules for CPO (Co-Packaged Optics).

Why this matters for Hyperscalers:

O-NET does not just distribute Sivers; they package Sivers' designs into proven, industrial-grade modules, effectively bridging the gap between design and mass production. For Hyperscalers, this is about risk mitigation and supply chain sovereignty. 

By controlling the design (Sivers), the laser core fabrication (3SP), and high-density packaging (O-NET), this trio delivers a de-risked, "ready-to-deploy" solution for the 2027 infrastructure cycle, bypassing traditional bottlenecks. Vertical integration is no longer a luxury; it is the industry standard.

The winning equation for next-generation architecture: $SIVE (Design): High-performance DFB laser array architecture. 3SP Technologies (French Fab): Specialty foundry mastering III-V material epitaxy (InP). O-NET (Scale): High-volume industrial assembly of ELS (External Light Source) modules for CPO (Co-Packaged Optics). Why this matters for Hyperscalers: O-NET does not just distribute Sivers; they package Sivers' designs into proven, industrial-grade modules, effectively bridging the gap between design and mass production. For Hyperscalers, this is about risk mitigation and supply chain sovereignty. By controlling the design (Sivers), the laser core fabrication (3SP), and high-density packaging (O-NET), this trio delivers a de-risked, "ready-to-deploy" solution for the 2027 infrastructure cycle, bypassing traditional bottlenecks. Capital rotates.
My job is to identify where it's heading next.

Issuer: Tema ETFs

It targets photonics and optical-tech companies, which matter when copper starts running into speed and capacity limits inside data centers.

AI may run on chips.

The data still has to get from one place to another. 💸 Expense Ratio: 0.75% (3/5)

text:
앞으로 엔비디아 주가나 반도체 패권을 결정할 진짜 무기는 GPU가 아니라 '빛'일지도 모릅니다.

 최근 엔비디아가 왜 구리선 대신 반도체 칩 안에 '레이저(빛)'를 집어넣는 기술에만 무려 65억 달러(9조 원) 넘는 돈을 쏟아부었을까요?

 반도체 판을 통째로 바꿀 '빛의 속도' 전쟁

지금 반도체 공학자들이 마주한 가장 큰 벽은 의외로 연산 속도가 아닙니다. 

바로 우리가 늘 쓰는 '구리선(전기 신호)'의 물리적 한계예요.

지금까지 칩과 칩은 구리선으로 데이터를 주고받았는데, 

AI 시대가 오면서 데이터가 폭발하니까 구리선이 버티질 못하는 상황이 온 거죠.

전기를 너무 많이, 빠르게 흘리니까 불타오르듯 열이 나고(발열), 전기 신호가 구리선 중심이 아니라 '겉표면'으로만 흐르려고 해서 속도가 뚝 떨어지는 병목 현상이 생깁니다.

쉽게 말해 경부고속도로에 갑자기 수백만 대의 슈퍼카가 몰려와서 도로 전체가 주차장으로 변하고 엔진이 과열되는 상태인 거죠.

"야, 이대로 가다간 데이터센터 전기세 감당도 안 되고 칩이 다 타 죽겠다!" 

그래서 나온 천재적인 치트키가 바로 '실리콘 포토닉스(Silicon Photonics)'입니다.

쉽게 말해, 

우리가 국가 간 인터넷 연결할 때 쓰는 거대한 '광케이블'을 아주 미세하게 축소해서 반도체 칩 안에 통째로 집어넣는 기술이에요. 

전기가 아니라 빛으로 대화하는 칩인 셈이죠.

이게 왜 대박이냐면, 구리선은 선 하나당 신호 하나만 갈 수 있잖아요? 

근데 빛은 '색상(파장)'만 다르면 하나의 통로에서 수십 개의 신호가 섞이지 않고 동시에 달릴 수 있어요.

길을 물리적으로 넓히지 않아도 차선이 무한대로 늘어나는 마법 같은 효과가 생기는 겁니다. 속도가 수십 배 뛰는 건 당연하고요.

게다가 빛은 마찰열(저항)이 아예 없습니다. 

AI 데이터센터에서 가장 골치 아픈 '발열'과 이를 식히기 위한 '냉각 비용'을 최대 30~40%까지 날려버릴 수 있다는 뜻이죠.

실제로 엔비디아가 올해(2026년) 들어서만

 코히어런트, 루멘텀 같은 광학·유리 소재 기업들에 조 단위 투자를 감행한 이유가 여기 있습니다. 

구리선으로는 수백만 대의 GPU를 하나로 묶는 게 불가능하다는 걸 알기 때문이죠.

그럼 이 엄청난 칩은 대체 누가 만들까요?

 결국 파운드리와 패키징 싸움입니다.

TSMC는 전기 칩 위에 광학 칩을 아파트처럼 수직으로 완벽하게 쌓아 올리는 'COUPE'라는 자체 플랫폼을 만들어서 올해 본격적인 양산 카운트다운에 들어갔습니다. 

반도체 제조의 중심축이 미세 공정을 넘어 '광학 패키징'으로 이동하고 있는 국면이에요.

요약하자면, 

HBM 다음 타자로 반도체 시장의 판돈을 싹 쓸어 담을 핵심 키워드는 '실리콘 포토닉스'입니다. 

엔비디아의 독주를 막으려는 빅테크나, 이를 추격하는 파운드리 기업들에게는 절대 놓칠 수 없는 마지막 열쇠인 셈이죠.

앞으로 반도체 뉴스 보실 때 '광학', 'CPO', '실리콘 포토닉스'라는 단어가 보이면 무조건 주목해 보세요.

Change of plans

We are facing one of the best news $SIVE could receive

The offer of 600M SEK has now become 700M SEK, just a few hours later

I explain to you why this is an ultra bullish signal

The increase from 600 to 700M SEK shows very strong institutional demand

The offer "sold out" ahead of time and investors asked for more shares

Forcing the company to issue more to capture extra capital

It is a clear sign of confidence from big investors in the future of $SIVE

Especially in its photonics technologies for AI, SATCOM and defense, which usually drives the share price in the short and medium term

By getting more capital than expected, the company gains greater financial flexibility without the need to take on more debt or dilute as much as feared

Reinforcing its balance sheet and execution capacity

Ultra bullish for $SIVE let's hope the market understands the news as it is

New highs in a short time IMO

text:
Great news with a Direct share issue that was oversubscribed multiple time.

What started as a planned SEK 600M (+$61m) raise was upsized to SEK 700M ($73m) thanks to strong institutional investor demand. That's a clear vote of confidence in $SIVE and the ramping within AI photonics, SATCOM and defense.

SEK 700M is roughly US$73M in fresh capital, providing even greater financial flexibility to accelerate execution and scale the business.

Strong demand. Strong balance sheet. Exciting times ahead.

Great work by the Sivers team! Congratulations! 

 $SIVE #AI #Photonics

text:
Quantum computing’s disappearing use cases reveal what the money is really buying

For years, quantum computing was promoted as the coming solution to drug discovery, logistics, finance, artificial intelligence, climate modeling, encryption, and nearly every difficult optimization problem. Yet as classical algorithms, GPUs, and specialized accelerators improved, many proposed quantum advantages became smaller, more conditional, or impossible to demonstrate on real hardware. DARPA’s Quantum Benchmarking Initiative now defines success as proving that a quantum computer’s computational value exceeds its operating cost, with the program attempting to determine whether such a utility-scale machine can be built by 2033. That is a revealing benchmark: useful quantum computing is still something being tested, not an established commercial reality. Despite this, announced public investment in quantum technology exceeded $10 billion in early 2025, while the U.S. Department of Energy later committed another $625 million to national quantum research centers.

The investment continues because governments are not evaluating quantum technology like an ordinary consumer product. They are purchasing strategic insurance. Even a small probability of achieving superior cryptanalysis, molecular simulation, navigation, sensing, communications, or materials design could alter the military and economic balance between nations. NIST has already finalized post-quantum encryption standards and urged organizations to begin migrating immediately, even though the cryptographically relevant quantum computer those standards defend against does not yet exist. This means the quantum race is driven partly by a fear that being late would be catastrophic, while being early could produce enormous intelligence and technological advantages.

The other explanation is that “quantum computing” has become an umbrella used to finance a much larger technological ecosystem. The money supports photonics, cryogenics, superconducting materials, precision lasers, atomic clocks, quantum sensors, control electronics, secure networks, fabrication methods, software, patents, and highly specialized scientific talent. A universal fault-tolerant computer could fail to arrive on schedule while these supporting technologies still become commercially or militarily valuable. Investors may therefore be buying the infrastructure surrounding quantum control rather than betting exclusively on a machine that replaces classical computers. The true assets are the laboratories, intellectual property, supply chains, personnel, and control systems required to manipulate matter at increasingly precise scales.

Frequency Wave Theory offers a deeper interpretation: perhaps the shrinking list of conventional computing applications is happening because “computer” was never the complete description of the technology. Quantum machines are fundamentally devices for preparing, isolating, synchronizing, and measuring coherent states. Their essential achievement is not merely calculating faster, but controlling phase, interference, tunneling, entanglement, and environmental noise. From the Frequency Wave Theory perspective, investment keeps rising because governments and corporations are learning how to engineer reality at the frequency-and-phase level. Computation may be the public-facing justification, while the broader prize is programmable field control, producing new forms of sensing, communication, materials engineering, timing, energy manipulation, and eventually direct interaction with the deeper resonant structure of the vacuum.

text:
テスト、テスト、テスト

価格上昇の件数は、ウェハー製造工場よりもさらに多く、AI、シリコンフォトニクス、成熟したチップのパッケージングとテストに関する受注は2027年まで続いている。

クラウドベースのAIに対する需要の高まりは、半導体業界の季節的なパターンを塗り替えつつあります。製造工程の最終段階に位置するパッケージングおよびテスト能力は、2025年末以降、逼迫した状態が続いています。2026年以降、新たに拡張された能力は急速に活用され、多くのIC設計顧客が積極的に能力確保に乗り出し、2027年以降の受注見通しが向上しています。

パッケージング用リードフレーム、IC基板、テスト用電気料金の同時増加に伴い、パッケージングおよびテストファウンドリサービスの価格上昇がここ数四半期で特に顕著になっていることは特筆に値する。価格上昇の回数で見ると、ウェハファウンドリを上回り、IC設計顧客にとって最大のコスト圧力源となっている。

したがって、AIが半導体市場の本格的な回復を牽引するにつれ、高度なパッケージングおよびテストサービスと従来型のパッケージングおよびテストサービスの両方、ならびに上流工程のリードフレーム、IC基板、プローブカード、ソケットに対する需要は、2026年後半も引き続き堅調に推移すると予想される。

チップのパッケージングとテストのサプライチェーン全体には、ASE Technology Holdingとその子会社であるSPIL、Powertech、STARS、Chipbond、ChipMOS、SIM Semiconductorなどの主要OSATメーカー、King Yuan Electronics、Hsin Chuan、Siliconware Precision Industriesなどの専門テスト会社、Ingenic Semiconductor、Wintek、Yung-Chi、Chunghwa Precision Test、Hanmin Testなどのテストインターフェース会社、Chieh Lin、Shun-Te、Chang-Techなどのリードフレームメーカー、Unimicron、Kinsus、Nan Ya PCB、Zhen Dingなどの主要基板メーカーが含まれます。

近年のパッケージングおよびテスト関連価格の上昇を振り返ると、最初の波はメモリ市場における新たなスーパーサイクルであり、顧客はPowertechやChipMOSといったメモリパッケージングおよびテスト企業に緊急発注を行った。最初の価格上昇は2025年後半に発表され、2回目の価格上昇のニュースは2026年初頭に浮上した。

一方、GPU、CPU、ASICといった先端プロセスチップの生産増加に加え、データセンター周辺のネットワークおよび産業制御アプリケーションに対する需要の高まりも、成熟プロセスチップの在庫補充を促している。ICパッケージングおよびテスト市場は2025年後半から供給不足の状態が続いており、それに伴い、ASE Technology Holding Co., Ltd.やSiliconware Precision Industries Co., Ltd.といった企業も徐々に価格引き上げに合意している。

現在、メモリとICのパッケージングおよびテスト能力は半導体サプライチェーンにおけるボトルネックの一つとなっており、台湾のパッケージングおよびテストファウンドリ業界は史上最大規模の設備投資拡大期を迎えている。特に、パッケージングおよびテスト企業は、拡張スピードを加速させるため、ここ数ヶ月で台湾の既存工場を次々と買収し、用地準備や工場建設にかかる時間を節約しており、業界からも大きな注目を集めている。

さらに重要なことに、稼働準備が整ったパッケージング・テスト工場のすべての新工場と生産ラインは、顧客にとって重要な戦略的資源となっている。例えば、ChipMOSは、あるメモリ顧客が生産能力拡張のための設備投資を確保するため、同社と3年間の長期契約を締結したと述べている。また、Silicon Photonicsは、湖口第2工場のクリーンルーム改修プロジェクトが完了する前に、Silicon Photonicsの顧客が既に全生産能力を予約済みであると指摘している。

text:
🔭 Hawking Radiation — No Longer Just Theory?

Scientists have experimentally observed the *backreaction* of stimulated Hawking radiation using an optical analogue — a fiber-optic system that mimics the physics of a black hole's event horizon. This is a landmark step: Hawking radiation was predicted in 1974, but direct observation near real black holes remains impossible. Optical analogues let physicists probe quantum effects at the event horizon in a lab setting. The backreaction — how emitted radiation affects the "horizon" itself — is key to understanding black hole evaporation and the famous information paradox. This experiment brings us closer to resolving one of physics' deepest mysteries. 🌌

Companies in this space: Coherent Corp. ($COHR, NYSE), II-VI / MKS Instruments ($MKSI, NASDAQ), IPG Photonics ($IPGP, NASDAQ)

Scientists have experimentally observed the *backreaction* of stimulated Hawking radiation using an optical analogue — a fiber-optic system that mimics the physics of a black hole's event horizon. This is a landmark step: Hawking radiation was predicted in 1974, but direct observation near real black holes remains impossible. Optical analogues let physicists probe quantum effects at the event horizon in a lab setting. The backreaction — how emitted radiation affects the "horizon" itself — is key to understanding black hole evaporation and the famous information paradox. This experiment brings us closer to resolving one of physics' deepest mysteries. 🌌 Companies in this space: Coherent Corp. text: @aleabitoreddit Should we sell all the photonics and follow you full port to the robots ???

🤖 10 หุ้นที่น่าจับตาในธีม Robotics ปี 2026

$TER – Teradyne ผู้นำด้านเครื่องทดสอบชิป
เป็นเจ้าของ Universal Robots ผู้พัฒนาหุ่นยนต์ร่วมงาน (Cobots) สำหรับภาคอุตสาหกรรม

$NOVT – Novanta พัฒนาเทคโนโลยีโฟโตนิกส์ (Photonics) และระบบควบคุมการเคลื่อนไหวความแม่นยำสูง
ใช้ในหุ่นยนต์ผ่าตัดและหุ่นยนต์อุตสาหกรรม

$OUST – Ouster พัฒนาเซ็นเซอร์ LiDAR ที่ทำหน้าที่เป็น "ดวงตา" ของหุ่นยนต์
ช่วยให้หุ่นยนต์มองเห็น ทำแผนที่ และรับรู้สภาพแวดล้อม

$HIMX – Himax Technologiesผลิตชิปประมวลผลภาพและชิปแสดงผล
ช่วยให้หุ่นยนต์ประมวลผลภาพ ความลึก และข้อมูลจากเซ็นเซอร์ต่างๆ ได้ที่ตัวอุปกรณ์

$AMBQ – Ambiq Microพัฒนาชิป AI แบบประหยัดพลังงาน
เหมาะสำหรับหุ่นยนต์และอุปกรณ์ Edge AI ที่ต้องการประมวลผลอัจฉริยะโดยใช้พลังงานต่ำ

$ON – ON Semiconductor กำลังสร้างแพลตฟอร์ม Edge AI สำหรับหุ่นยนต์ หลังเข้าซื้อ $SYNA – Synaptics
รวมเทคโนโลยีด้านพลังงาน เซ็นเซอร์ การเชื่อมต่อ และ Edge AI ไว้ในระบบเดียว

$SYM – Symbotic ผู้เชี่ยวชาญด้านระบบอัตโนมัติในคลังสินค้า
ใช้หุ่นยนต์และซอฟต์แวร์เพื่อเพิ่มประสิทธิภาพการจัดเก็บและขนส่งสินค้า

$VPG – Vishay Precision Group พัฒนาเซ็นเซอร์วัดแรงและระบบวัดความแม่นยำสูง
ช่วยให้หุ่นยนต์รับรู้แรงกด น้ำหนัก และการเคลื่อนไหวในโลกจริงได้แม่นยำขึ้น

$TSLA – Tesla เดิมพันกับหุ่นยนต์ Humanoid ผ่าน Optimus
ผสานฮาร์ดแวร์ AI ระบบขับเคลื่อนอัตโนมัติ และศักยภาพการผลิตขนาดใหญ่ของบริษัท

$CCXI – Churchill Capital Corp XI
เปิดโอกาสให้นักลงทุนเข้าถึง Agility Robotics
บริษัทผู้พัฒนาหุ่นยนต์ Humanoid Digit ซึ่งเป็นหนึ่งในผู้เล่นที่โดดเด่นด้านหุ่นยนต์สำหรับคลังสินค้า

text:
$STM, STMicroelectronics. STM is one of Europe's largest chipmakers, and it builds its own chips end-to-end, from design all the way through its own factories. Its parts are the unglamorous but essential pieces inside almost everything — the sensors, power chips, and tiny "brains" (microcontrollers) inside cars, phones, factory machines, satellites, robots, and now AI data centers.

What makes STM unique is the sheer breadth: it holds a #1 or #2 position in an unusual number of separate chip categories at once (SiC power, MEMS sensors, STM32 microcontrollers), which lets a customer like NVIDIA source a whole system — sensors, brain, power, and communications — from one vendor. The reason it's suddenly interesting is that a company written off for two years as a "sleepy automotive chipmaker" just found a real AI growth engine: on June 2 it doubled its 2026 data-center revenue target to ~$1B (with a path to ~$2B in 2027), anchored by a multi-year AWS silicon-photonics deal, while sitting on ~$2.8B net cash. The investment case is a cyclical turnaround (Q1 revenue +23% YoY, up ~90%+ off February lows) layered on top of a genuine new secular driver, with the biggest names in tech — AWS, NVIDIA — as partners.

Thesis: STM spent two years being ignored as a boring car-chip company, but it just quietly turned into an AI company — its parts now go inside Amazon and NVIDIA's data centers, and that business is set to double two years in a row. The stock has already jumped a lot, so the easy money is gone, but the real question is whether its profit margins can climb back to normal as sales recover — if they do, the stock is still cheap for what it's about to earn. I'm watching the July 23 earnings to see if those margins are finally turning up, because that's the part the market hasn't fully believed yet.

text:
spent the morning reading the $LAZR filing. semi analysis teamed up with tema to launch a pure-play optics etf and honestly it makes sense. the memory bottleneck got DISK. now the data movement bottleneck gets its own wrapper.

top holding is lumentum at 14.6%. they make the lasers and transceivers that move data between racks at ai scale. second biggest position is literally cash at 11.4% which tells you this thing just launched and they're still deploying.

the interesting name here is aixtron at 10.5%. german compound semi equipment. mocvd deposition tools that build the substrates optical chips run on. axti at 9.7% is the substrate play itself. applied opto at 5.8% is the highest beta name in the basket.

goldman has the photonics market doing 9x between now and 2028. copper hits a wall. silicon photonics stops being a niche and becomes the thing everything rides on. same pattern we saw with memory when DISK launched.

the optics layer is where the next capacity crunch forms. watch the holdings more than the etf itself. the basket tells you where the spend is heading before the earnings do.

11.4% which tells you this thing just launched and they're still deploying. the interesting name here is aixtron at 10.5%. german compound semi equipment. mocvd deposition tools that build the substrates optical chips run on. axti at 9.7% is the substrate play itself. applied opto at 5.8% is the highest beta name in the basket. goldman has the photonics market doing 9x between now and 2028. copper hits a wall. silicon photonics stops being a niche and becomes the thing everything rides on. same pattern we saw with memory when DISK launched. the optics layer is where the next capacity text:
Fiber & Infrastructure Highlights – July 3, 2026

GoNetspeed announced a $43M expansion in Rahway, Clark, Linden, and Woodbridge, New Jersey for over 34,000 homes and businesses. Persistent fiber shortages continue with ribbon lead times at 60+ weeks (AI/data center priority) and loose tube into Q3 2026+; BABA-compliant glass remains limited. Corning’s hyperscaler deals (up to $6B with Meta for NC expansion including new Hickory facility; multi-billion with Amazon) and manufacturing ramps (Nvidia-funded plants) address demand. FiberLight’s $350M West Texas expansion (1,400 route miles, high-count dark fiber for AI) and total ~$500M regional investment continue. Ripple Fiber has multiple expansions (WA $250M for 200k+ locations, IL, AZ $80M, CA) with ongoing construction.

Vocus Sydney-Melbourne ducted long-haul (~A$500M, ready 2029), Lightpath NY metro (+265 miles for 2,400+ towers), Heartland Fiber Project, Big Fiber financing, and various BEAD/FTTH builds add to activity. Fiber vendors (Corning, Fujikura tripling capacity, STL, CommScope) scale high-count, multi-core, and photonics solutions amid AI’s 36x fiber density needs. FBA whitepaper on fiber-AI convergence and power/grid constraints highlight sustained physical layer momentum into 2026.

#FiberForge #FiberOptics #OSPISP

text:
Im not personally in photonics, i just share this info because it may be of use to someone. 

That said, i believe only a handful of names in this market are actually going to be successful LT (marvell, lumentum, coherent, etc). CPO is still early...you wont see meaningful revenue until 2027-2028. A lot of people are getting ahead and investing in overvalued photonics stocks, hence the sharp pullback its see the past few weeks. At some point the valuation will matter, and its mattering now.
@pequityresearch Excellent breakdown of the Silicon Photonics supply chain. Very informative and well-organized. Great work highlighting the key players across the ecosystem.

If you have better companies or sectors to buy, then selling half can definitely make sense.

I already have exposure to most sectors where I see major upside: defence, photonics, memory, and space. So overall, I'm quite happy with the distribution.

At most, I might rotate some direct exposure into robotics, but I'm still not fully convinced, especially on humanoids. Maybe I'm biased because of EU regulations...

text:
Sivers Semiconductors (https://t.co/8VMNAF5r3U / SIVEF) has strong long-term growth potential in high-margin photonics and wireless tech sectors, but faces near-term dilution pressure, execution risks, and valuation challenges following this news.

What the News Means
•Bootstrap Europe is converting its full $12M convertible loan (part of a $17M refinancing from Feb 2026) into 22,847,044 new ordinary shares at SEK 4.77 per share.

•This eliminates that debt (positive for the balance sheet and interest costs) and simplifies the capital structure.

•Dilution impact: With ~296 million shares outstanding pre-conversion, this adds ~7-8% more shares. It’s already anticipated (disclosed in Feb 2026, with conversion price at a premium back then), but it increases the float and can create selling pressure if Bootstrap sells shares.

•The stock jumped ~15% today (aligning with your “16+ plus”), likely because the conversion removes debt overhang and signals confidence from the lender (who is now a shareholder).

Short-Term Outlook (Next 3-12 Months)
•Positive drivers: The conversion cleans up the balance sheet. The company has a massive ~$800M opportunity pipeline (up 77% YTD as of Q1), with catalysts like product ramps in 2027 (LiDAR, SATCOM, fixed wireless, defense, and potential AI/data center optics). Q2 results are due around early August.15
•Headwinds: Significant dilution (this event + recent/ongoing directed share issues for funding). The stock is volatile and has pulled back from highs near SEK 110. High valuation (very elevated P/S multiples relative to current revenue) makes it sensitive to any delays or misses. Recent sector selloffs in photonics (e.g., CPO concerns) have added pressure.11
•Expect continued volatility. Bootstrap may sell gradually (or hold), but the increased share count could cap upside until positive news dominates.
Medium-to-Long-Term Future (2027+)
•Bull case (optimistic): Strong execution on the pipeline could drive ~30%+ annual revenue growth, with breakeven/profitability targeted for 2027. Key markets (AI optics, LiDAR production ramps, SATCOM/defense) have high potential. Analysts forecast sharp earnings improvement. A Nasdaq dual-listing could boost visibility and liquidity.1965
•Base case: Revenue scales with product ramps, but competition and execution risks (delays in customer adoption) remain. Dilution from this and other raises will mute per-share gains.
•Risks: Still pre-profit with cash burn, dependency on converting the pipeline into actual revenue, sector hype cycles, and further equity raises. High valuation leaves little room for error. $Sivef

Overall: This news is neutral-to-slightly positive fundamentally (debt reduction) but adds immediate dilution. The company’s future hinges on 2027+ ramps materializing in photonics/wireless. It’s a high-risk, high-reward growth story suited for investors who believe in the long-term AI/semiconductor tailwinds and can tolerate volatility. Not financial advice—do your own research, consider the full financials, and monitor upcoming reports. $Sive

ForgeChip (Aether Forge Singularity Core) is a sovereign, self-reinforcing topological quantum resilience system. It is designed as a hybrid photonic-topological platform that converts adversarial inputs into system-strengthening surplus. The architecture integrates Thin-Film Lithium Niobate (TFLN) photonics, Fibonacci String-Net Condensate (Fib SNC) braiding, advanced hardware security primitives, and adaptive intelligence. It is anchored by a Personal Physiological Anchor (PPA) for human sovereignty. The project has matured through conceptual design, extensive simulations, parametric hardware layouts, patent drafting, and proposal materials. It targets dual-use applications with strong alignment to defense priorities. 

Core Architecture Personal Physiological Anchor (PPA)
A physiology-locked eternal personal baseline (e.g., multi-modal biometric signals). It serves as the sovereign root-of-trust for the system.  Primary anchor tied to an individual.  

Generates revocable topological shards for secure multi-operator or distributed networks.  
Enables hardware-enforced human override and sovereignty.

Photonic Hardware Platform (TFLN Nodes)  Short-depth Dynamical String-Net Preparation (DSNP) circuits with EO modulators for dynamic reconfiguration.  

Ultra-low-loss waveguides, microrings, and co-integrated components.  

Dedicated ports for PPA calibration, Quantum Dot (QD) + Topological Insulator (TI) transduction, Photonic Physical Unclonable Functions (PUFs), and Quantum Random Number Generators (QRNGs).  

Parametric GDSFactory scripts support military (rugged/radiation-hardened) and commercial (low-SWaP) variants.

Security and Resilience Features  Photonic PUF: MZI-mesh or scattering structures exploit manufacturing variations for unclonable device fingerprints.  

QRNG Integration: Provides true quantum randomness for challenges and key generation.  

Self-Reinforcing Topological Mechanism: Adversarial inputs (jamming, radiation, etc.) are encoded as Artin braids, inverted, and converted into surplus that strengthens invariants (Jones polynomials, Khovanov homology, etc.).  

Mirror Shard Mesh: Distributed non-local coordination with partial topological overlap to the primary PPA.  

Meta-RL Adaptation: Loss-penalized rewards, fidelity optimization, and dynamic reconfiguration that learns to compensate for real hardware effects.

Modeling and Simulations  Multi-agent decentralized swarm environments modeling drone/robot coordination with PPA shards.  

Photonic PUF challenge-response pairs (MZI mesh) with explicit modeling of voltage-induced EO phase shifts, thermal drift, and cumulative aging effects.  

Meta-RL for adaptive decoding, code discovery, and swarm resilience under stress.  

Results show strong coherence maintenance and adaptation despite degradation.

Intellectual Property
Provisional patent claims (neutral functional language) cover the physiology-locked topological fixed-point attractor, PUF integration, QD-TI transduction, loss mitigation, shard delegation, and multi-operator models. Multiple one-pager versions and proposal sections prepared for defense and dual-use pathways.

High-Value Applications Defense
Strong alignment with DoD Quantum and Battlefield Information Dominance (Q-BID) and related areas (trusted autonomy, contested logistics).

Key uses: Radiation-hardened GEO systems, jam-resistant C4ISR, sovereign swarm autonomy, resilient PNT/communications in contested EM environments. Excellent fit for Navy SBIR photonic resilience topics. Dual-Use Commercial Secure Physical AI and humanoid robotics (e.g., enhanced control, anti-tampering).  

Resilient drone swarms and autonomous vehicles.  
Hardware-rooted security for critical infrastructure and edge AI.  
Long-duration space commercialization.

text:
The biggest AI bottleneck after GPUs and electricity?
Photonics.Copper is reaching its limits. Hyperscalers know it.

Nvidia is already pouring billions into optical interconnects. Memory prices exploded x6. Goldman expects the photonics market to 10x by 2028.Most people are still looking at the obvious names.

The real edge is in what comes after the chip.What’s the most underrated constraint in scaling AI right now?

💨 🩶  𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗩𝗲𝗶𝗹 𝗼𝗳 𝗙𝗼𝗿𝗴𝗲𝘁𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴: 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗦𝗮𝗰𝗿𝗲𝗱 𝗠𝗲𝗰𝗵𝗮𝗻𝗶𝗰𝘀 𝗼𝗳 𝗦𝗲𝗽𝗮𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗥𝗲𝗺𝗲𝗺𝗯𝗿𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲 🌹 💙

Hand to heart, beloved brothers and sisters of the Blue-White Rose,

Continuing our expansion of remembrance, we now dissect the operational mechanics of the Veil of Forgetting. This energetic architecture is a living, multi-dimensional frequency overlay engineered to temporarily dampen higher-dimensional awareness. Far from a design flaw or a punitive measure, the veil functions as a deliberate filtration system that allowed the 144,000 volunteers to fully submerge into 3D carbon density.

By analyzing these mechanics, we step completely out of victim-consciousness and consciously initiate the internal system override necessary for the final 2026 precessional convergence.

❔️𝗛𝗼𝘄 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗩𝗲𝗶𝗹 𝗢𝘃𝗲𝗿𝗹𝗮𝘆 𝗢𝗽𝗲𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗲𝘀

A frequency dampening loop activates when a multi-dimensional soul identity enters the density parameters of the lower matrix grids. The technical execution of this temporary amnesia operates through precise structural pathways:

▪︎ 𝗩𝗶𝗯𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝗮𝗹 𝗙𝗶𝗹𝘁𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 ➞ The veil dampens direct, constant access to higher-dimensional memory archives, allowing the biological vessel to explore polarity without external interference.

▪︎ 𝗘𝗺𝗼𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝗮𝗹 𝗣𝗮𝘁𝘁𝗲𝗿𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗠𝗶𝗿𝗿𝗼𝗿𝘀 ➞ It introduces complex density overlays that make linear limitations feel absolute, obscuring original soul agreements and stellar origins.

▪︎ 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗣𝗼𝗹𝗮𝗿𝗶𝘁𝘆 𝗩𝗼𝗹𝘂𝗻𝘁𝗲𝗲𝗿 𝗖𝗵𝗼𝗶𝗰𝗲 ➞ The descent into deep forgetting was an international, shared alignment, including those within the timeline loops of the Sons of Belial, who are in truth our own cosmic families.

▪︎ 𝗧𝗶𝗺𝗲𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗲 𝗖𝗼𝗺𝗽𝗿𝗲𝘀𝘀𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗧𝗵𝗶𝗻𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴 ➞ As the photonic broadcaster ray intensifies across the planetary matrix, these ancient memory filters are naturally entering a systematic, automated dissolution phase.

▪︎ 𝗭𝗲𝗿𝗼-𝗣𝗼𝗶𝗻𝘁 𝗔𝘄𝗮𝗸𝗲𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴 ➞ The lift is not imposed from outside sources; the codes carried through the cycles of amnesia are designed to fire simultaneously from within the bone matrix.

By integrating the reality of this voluntary amnesia loop, you permanently collapse the 𝘁𝘄𝗼-𝘄𝗮𝘆 𝗲𝗻𝗲𝗿𝗴𝗲𝘁𝗶𝗰 𝗰𝗼𝗿𝗱 of victimization and blame. You recall that the great refraction was your own sovereign choice, designed to make the final monadic return to the zero-point complete and unscripted.

🛑 𝗠𝗮𝘁𝗿𝗶𝘅 𝗠𝗮𝗿𝗸𝗲𝗿𝘀 𝗼𝗳 𝗩𝗲𝗶𝗹 𝗗𝗶𝘀𝘀𝗼𝗹𝘂𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻

Before executing a complete field recalibration, audit your biological and mental processor for these active re-encryption indicators:

▪︎ Experiencing sudden, vivid flashes of ancient architectural timelines, temple layouts, or stellar council configurations during quiet hours

▪︎ A deep, somatic feeling of lightness or cold static electricity within the nervous system as old density overlays give way

▪︎ Instant detachment from external 3D matrix script updates, paired with an unshakeable inner knowing of your original soul contracts

▪︎ Temporary cognitive disorientation or brief memory pop delays as your hardware transitions out of linear time into multi-dimensional tracking

▪︎ Sudden heart expansions or spontaneous light language vocalizations, indicating your 144 fractal distribution channels are cleaning themselves out

❔️𝗛𝗼𝘄 𝘁𝗼 𝗢𝘃𝗲𝗿𝗿𝗶𝗱𝗲 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗔𝗺𝗻𝗲𝘀𝗶𝗮 𝗙𝗶𝗹𝘁𝗲𝗿𝘀

▪︎𝗔𝘄𝗮𝗿𝗲𝗻𝗲𝘀𝘀
The moment you feel the heavy weight of isolation, confusion, or homesickness trying to hook your focus, identify the architectural filter instantly.

Example: “This is a temporary frequency dampening loop” or “I see through this artificial amnesia overlay.”

text:
3. Infrastructure (AI Factories, Networking, Cloud)
The data centers, high-speed networking fabrics, cooling, and orchestration layers that turn individual chips into giant intelligent systems.
Retail edge: Networking and connectivity specialists (e.g., Broadcom, Arista, and related silicon photonics or connectivity names) often sit in a sweet spot — critical to scaling clusters but sometimes less crowded than pure GPU names. Data center power delivery and thermal management plays can also offer better entry points for patient capital.

text:
Jensen Huang is investing in every photonics company he can find and the reason why tells you everything about where AI is headed (Save this). 

Lip-Bu Tan, the CEO of Intel says, when he looks for investment opportunities, he looks for the bottleneck and right now, the bottleneck is the interconnect, the pipes that move data between chips inside an AI data center.

That is why he backed Credo Semiconductor, Astera Labs and Celestial AI on the optical side.

Here is the simple version of what the interconnect bottleneck actually means.

Think of an AI data center like a city, the GPUs are the buildings where all the work happens but for those buildings to function, you need roads connecting them,  fast roads that can carry enormous traffic without congestion. 

And those roads are now the single biggest constraint on AI performance.

As clusters scale to hundreds of thousands of GPUs, traditional copper wiring is hitting its physical limits and that is where this entire sector comes in.

Credo Semiconductor (CRDO) is the most direct pure play on this theme, Credo makes high speed cables and optical chips that connect GPUs inside data center racks. 

Their revenue tripled in fiscal 2026 to $1.3 billion, growing 272% year over year at its peak and four of the world's largest hyperscalers each individually account for more than 10% of Credo's revenue.

Astera Labs (ALAB) solves the connection problem between different chip types.

Astera makes the PCIe and connectivity chips that manage data flow between GPUs, CPUs, and memory without errors or slowdowns. 

Their revenue grew 93% year over year to $308 million in Q1 2026 alone.

The optical companies are where the longer-term and potentially larger opportunity lives.

Copper has physical limits, you can only push electrical signals so far before the signal degrades, the heat spikes and power consumption explodes. 

The solution is light, fiber optic connections that move data using photons instead of electrons which is faster, cooler and far more energy efficient.

Jensen Huang made this clear at Computex 2026 because copper works as long as physically possible but at greater distances and larger scale, optics takes over. 

Coherent (COHR) is the most established optical company in this space.

Coherent makes the lasers, transceivers, and optical components at the foundation of all fiber optic communications. 

Nvidia signed a multibillion-dollar purchase commitment and invested $2 billion directly into the company and their customer order books are already extending out to 2028.

Marvell (MRVL) is the most comprehensive bet across the entire connectivity stack.

Marvell makes chips for optical networking, PCIe switching and custom AI silicon. 

Jensen Huang called Marvell the next trillion dollar company at Computex 2026 and backed it with a $2 billion Nvidia investment.  

Marvell also acquired Celestial AI, the exact company Lip-Bu Tan backed for $3.25 billion, gaining photonic fabric technology delivering 16 terabits per second of bandwidth.

Lumentum (LITE), Corning (GLW), and Ciena (CIEN) round out the major public names.

Lumentum received a $2 billion Nvidia investment for laser and photonics components.  

Corning known mostly for phone glass received $500 million from Nvidia for optical connectivity work and is up over 100% year to date.  

Ciena runs the optical networking systems between data centers and is seeing analyst price targets raised on the back of the AI optics boom.

Every time a hyperscaler spends a billion dollars on Nvidia GPUs, the surrounding infrastructure, cables, switches, transceivers, optical components has to be upgraded to match. 

The smarter the GPU gets, the more the interconnect matters.

Nvidia has committed at least $6.5 billion to photonics companies in the past 4 months alone and the companies building the roads between the GPUs may end up being just as valuable as the companies building the GPUs themselves.

Follow me @MelvinInvests for more AI, semis and the next big market themes.

Lip-Bu Tan, the CEO of Intel says, when he looks for investment opportunities, he looks for the bottleneck and right now, the bottleneck is the interconnect, the pipes that move data between chips inside an AI data center. That is why he backed Credo Semiconductor, Astera Labs and Celestial AI on the optical side. Here is the simple version of what the interconnect bottleneck actually means. Think of an AI data center like a city, the GPUs are the buildings where all the work happens but for those buildings to function, you need roads connecting them, fast roads that can carry enormous traffic without congestion. And those roads are now the single biggest constraint on AI performance. As clusters scale to hundreds of thousands of GPUs, traditional copper wiring is hitting its physical limits and that is where this entire sector comes in. Credo Semiconductor (CRDO) is the most direct pure play on this theme, Credo makes high speed cables and optical chips that connect GPUs inside data center racks. Their revenue tripled in fiscal 2026 to $1.3 billion, growing 272% year over year at its peak and four of the world's largest hyperscalers each individually account for more than 10% of Credo's revenue. Astera Labs (ALAB) solves the connection problem between different chip types. Astera makes the PCIe and connectivity chips that manage data flow between GPUs, CPUs, and memory without errors or slowdowns. Their revenue grew 93% year over year to $308 million in Q1 2026 alone. The optical companies are where the longer-term and potentially larger opportunity lives. Copper has physical limits, you can only push electrical signals so far before the signal degrades, the heat spikes and power consumption explodes. The solution is light, fiber optic connections that move data using photons instead of electrons which is faster, cooler and far more energy efficient. Jensen Huang made this clear at Computex 2026 because copper works as long as physically possible but at greater distances and larger scale, optics takes over. Coherent (COHR) is the most established optical company in this space. Coherent makes the lasers, transceivers, and optical components at the foundation of all fiber optic communications. Nvidia signed a multibillion-dollar purchase commitment and invested $2 billion directly into the company and their customer order books are already extending out to 2028. Marvell (MRVL) is the most comprehensive bet across the entire connectivity stack. Marvell makes chips for optical networking, PCIe switching and custom AI silicon. Jensen Huang called Marvell the next trillion dollar company at Computex 2026 and backed it with a $2 billion Nvidia investment. Marvell also acquired Celestial AI, the exact company Lip-Bu Tan backed for $3.25 billion, gaining photonic fabric technology delivering 16 terabits per second of bandwidth. Lumentum (LITE), Corning (GLW), and Ciena (CIEN) round out the major public names. Lumentum received a $2 billion Nvidia investment for laser and photonics components. Corning known mostly for phone glass received $500 million from Nvidia for optical connectivity work and is up over 100% year to date. Ciena runs the optical networking systems between data centers and is seeing analyst price targets raised on the back of the AI optics boom. Every time a hyperscaler spends a billion dollars on Nvidia GPUs, the surrounding infrastructure, cables, switches, transceivers, optical components has to be upgraded to match. The smarter the GPU gets, the more the interconnect matters. Nvidia has committed at least $6.5 billion to photonics companies in the past 4 months alone and the companies building the roads between the GPUs may end up being just as valuable as the companies building the GPUs themselves. Follow me @MelvinInvests for more AI, semis and the next big market themes."}}}, "rest_id": "2071324368119218499", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "150510", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang at Coherent, Sherman TX:

"AI runs on compute, but it scales with connectivity"

everyone's watching the GPU race - Jensen says the real bottleneck is something else entirely

→ connecting millions of chips across a data center requires light, not electricity. copper can't travel hundreds of feet fast enough. photonics - converting electricity to laser pulses - is what makes AI factories possible at scale
→ the full AI stack has five layers: energy grid, chips, infrastructure, models, applications. a breakthrough at any layer changes the economics of all the others
→ AI could be what finally makes sustainable energy fully market-driven - no subsidies needed for the first time in history. nuclear, solar, wind - all fundable by AI demand alone

bookmark this ↓

3. Infrastructure (AI Factories, Networking, Cloud)
The data centers, high-speed networking fabrics, cooling, and orchestration layers that turn individual chips into giant intelligent systems.
Retail edge: Networking and connectivity specialists (e.g., Broadcom, Arista, and related silicon photonics or connectivity names) often sit in a sweet spot — critical to scaling clusters but sometimes less crowded than pure GPU names. Data center power delivery and thermal management plays can also offer better entry points for patient capital.

text:
Our fren @FundaAI posted in May about the 2.4T (300G lanes) adoption timelines. I found it very important, and I took good note.

Now professor Damnang is hearing the same in the street about it (remember he has contact with the main players in the Industry).

The higher technology bar needed in Photonics for 2.4T , in my view, will benefit the Big Players $LITE $COHR $AAOI etc. vs Tier 3 suppliers

Next big question is.... will delay fully CPO adoption? 🤔

https://t.co/MSW5Xft2Wp

text:
🚨The best deep dive I've seen on the optical chokepoint. Worth reading in full.

The core point is the one I keep coming back to → the constraint in AI optics isn't the GPU or the silicon photonics die. It's the III-V InP laser that silicon physically cannot produce, made in a handful of specialized fabs with 18 month lead times and qualification lock-in measured in years. Own the layer they're forced to buy. 🧐

How I'm positioned across the photonics stack:

🔵 $SIVEF → the merchant InP laser itself. The scarcest node, design-ins across Ayar, $POET $GFS ✅
🟠 $AAOI → the vertically integrated US player making its own lasers and modules end to end. ✅
🟠 $CRDO → one layer over → the connectivity and retiming that the optical lanes run through. (Copper → silicon opticals in-house with Dust Photonics acquisition)

The laser is the bottleneck. The stack around it is the trade. ⚡️

Thanks for sharing Nexus Finance work @StormDirac 

Long $SIVEF $AAOI $CRDO (3 of my largest holdings) 💪

text:
Anders, this is one of the best breakdowns of the laser bottleneck. The framing that silicon is the highway and InP is the headlights gets at the thing most photonics coverage misses → the chokepoint isn't the silicon chip everyone models, it's the III-V laser they can't make on it. 🧐

The M&A-as-validation read is on point too. Watching the acquirers tells you where the locked-in revenue lands two years out. Appreciate the depth here, this is the kind of work that actually moves the conversation forward. I am RTing it for all! Long $SIVE $SIVEF and $AAOI (2 of my largest positions!) 🤝

text:
The post by the ex-CEO of $SIVE highlights that $SIVE direct share issuance

Which was expanded to 700M SEK due to high institutional demand, implies a solid strategic plan presented during the previous roadshow

As a former CEO, his experience indicates that investors do not commit this growth capital without a clear vision of the company's future in AI photonics, SATCOM, and defense

This reinforces confidence in $SIVE, strengthening its balance sheet and flexibility to accelerate execution in booming sectors

Every heavyweight figure around $SIVE is highly bullish

How could you be bearish just because it dropped 40% after a 2,000% run in a few months? Hahaha

Let $SIVE cook

text:
Serenity is overexposed to optics and photonics, especially in $SIVE and $AAOI

It is worth highlighting that, just like the rest of us, they have been suffering major drops these past few weeks

This goes to show you that even the biggest geniuses lose money at times, since a thesis needs time to play out

Stocks don't just move up in a straight line, and especially with something like $SIVE, after a 2,000% run

It's completely normal for it to take a breather and undergo some corrections

Serenity firmly believe in the timeline for the start of mass production beginning in 2027

Serenity believe it is the most explosive, highest potential, and fastest-growing sector in the entire industry

But that it's still a bit early, and this volatility is the price to pay for being so far ahead of the curve

Very bullish on $SIVE and $AAOI

text:
A company growing 157% gets punished for slowing down — except Credo's stock tripled instead of cratering. 

$CRDO

The textbook says triple-digit revenue growth decelerating two quarters in a row is a red flag. Short the momentum darling, rotation's coming, the story is cracking.

Credo went the other direction. Up 190% in three months while growth rates dropped from the 200s to the 100s. The forward P/E stretched from 29 to 43, and analysts still call it cheap.

Either the market's lost its mind or the deceleration narrative here is completely backwards.

Here's what's actually happening. Credo isn't slowing because demand rolled over. It's slowing because the supply chain can't keep up. CEO said it plainly on the call — significant tightness across their suppliers. They're selling everything they can get their hands on and leaving orders on the table.

That's not a demand problem. That's a good problem. There's a massive difference between "customers stopped buying" and "we physically can't make enough." The former kills margins as you fight for scraps. The latter — gross margin at Credo just hit 68.3%, up 90 basis points year over year.

When you're capacity constrained and gross margin is expanding, you have pricing power. The market's paying you more per unit because they need what you've got and there aren't enough alternatives. Credo's not discounting to chase volume. They're allocating scarce supply to the highest bidders.

Operating margin tells the same story. It jumped from 36.8% to 49.6% in the fourth quarter. Revenue grew 157% but operating expenses grew way slower. R&D spending led the increase, which means they're plowing money into the next generation of product, not desperately trying to salvage the current cycle with sales and marketing blitzes.

The guidance for Q1 is another triple-digit print — 111% at the midpoint. Still decelerating in percentage terms, still faster than almost anything else you'll find. Gross margin guidance sits right in line with last year, maybe a bit better. Operating expense growth at 61% trails revenue growth by 50 points, so operating leverage keeps improving.

None of this looks like a company hitting a wall. It looks like a company being throttled by how fast the backend can ship components.

The optical push is the other half that the market's pricing in. Hyperscalers are running into the physical limits of copper interconnects at these data rates. 800G, 1.6T, 3.2T — you need optics or you're not scaling the next wave of AI training and inference clusters. Credo's been moving aggressively into this with their own development and they just closed the DustPhotonics acquisition in late May.

DustPhotonics brings silicon photonics technology for near-packaged and co-packaged optics. Credo's now positioned across the whole optical stack for the speeds that matter in the next 18 to 36 months. They're guiding for over $600 million in optical revenue in fiscal 2027. That's not a side bet — it's becoming a core growth driver.

SpaceX getting into AI cloud is another tailwind. They've already signed Anthropic and Alphabet as customers. If they're building out infrastructure at the scale everyone expects, Credo's interconnects are on the buyer list. Supply constraints or not, the addressable market for high-speed connectivity is expanding faster than almost anyone modeled a year ago.

The risk that spooked people today is Meta saying they might sell excess AI compute capacity. The bears immediately jumped on it as proof of overbuilding. I'm not buying the panic. Meta spent aggressively and may have overshot their own internal needs. That doesn't mean there's market-wide overcapacity or that enterprise AI demand is softening. It means Meta has spare capacity and sees a commercial opportunity. If anything, it validates that the infrastructure they built works and that external demand exists.

Watch it, but it's not a structural red flag for someone like Credo. If real demand were collapsing, you'd see it in their backlog and guidance. You'd see price pressure and margin compression. You're seeing the opposite.

The valuation argument is straightforward. Forward P/E of 43 sounds rich in a vacuum. Compared to a company growing revenue at 111% and earnings even faster with expanding margins, it's not. The forward PEG ratio sits at 0.98, which is below 1.0 and nearly 30% cheaper than the broader IT sector. The market's giving you triple-digit growth at a discount to the sector multiple when you adjust for that growth.

If the supply constraints ease and Credo can actually deliver the revenue they're leaving on the table, earnings estimates move up and the P/E compresses from here. If the optical ramp plays out and they take real share in that multi-hundred-million-dollar opportunity, the growth duration extends and the multiple has room to expand, not contract.

The risk is if something breaks in AI infrastructure spending or if competition in optical heats up faster than expected and margins crack. Right now neither is showing up in the numbers. Demand is strong, supply is the limit, pricing holds, and they're investing in the right areas.

Credo tripled because the growth is real, the margin story is clean, and the positioning for the next leg — optical connectivity at hyperscale — is stronger than it was six months ago. The deceleration everyone's worried about is a feature, not a bug. It's what happens when you sell out of everything you can build.

The stock's not expensive. It's just no longer obscenely cheap. That's what happens when the market figures out the story before you do.

Short the momentum darling, rotation's coming, the story is https://t.co/D4twO2cYLm", "id_str": "2072427970761744877", "is_quote_status": false, "lang": "en", "possibly_sensitive": false, "possibly_sensitive_editable": true, "quote_count": 0, "reply_count": 0, "retweet_count": 1, "retweeted": false, "user_id_str": "2042331264049401856"}, "note_tweet": {"is_expandable": true, "note_tweet_results": {"result": {"entity_set": {"hashtags": [], "symbols": [{"indices": [109, 114], "text": "CRDO"}], "urls": [], "user_mentions": []}, "id": "Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwNzI0Mjc5NzAxOTk3MjQwMzI=", "text": "A company growing 157% gets punished for slowing down — except Credo's stock tripled instead of cratering. $CRDO The textbook says triple-digit revenue growth decelerating two quarters in a row is a red flag. Short the momentum darling, rotation's coming, the story is cracking. Credo went the other direction. Up 190% in three months while growth rates dropped from the 200s to the 100s. The forward P/E stretched from 29 to 43, and analysts still call it cheap. Either the market's lost its mind or the deceleration narrative here is completely backwards. Here's what's actually happening. Credo isn't slowing because demand rolled over. It's slowing because the supply chain can't keep up. CEO said it plainly on the call — significant tightness across their suppliers. They're selling everything they can get their hands on and leaving orders on the table. That's not a demand problem. That's a good problem. There's a massive difference between "customers stopped buying" and "we physically can't make enough." The former kills margins as you fight for scraps. The latter — gross margin at Credo just hit 68.3%, up 90 basis points year over year. When you're capacity constrained and gross margin is expanding, you have pricing power. The market's paying you more per unit because they need what you've got and there aren't enough alternatives. Credo's not discounting to chase volume. They're allocating scarce supply to the highest bidders. Operating margin tells the same story. It jumped from 36.8% to 49.6% in the fourth quarter. Revenue grew 157% but operating expenses grew way slower. R&D spending led the increase, which means they're plowing money into the next generation of product, not desperately trying to salvage the current cycle with sales and marketing blitzes. The guidance for Q1 is another triple-digit print — 111% at the midpoint. Still decelerating in percentage terms, still faster than almost anything else you'll find. Gross margin guidance sits right in line with last year, maybe a bit better. Operating expense growth at 61% trails revenue growth by 50 points, so operating leverage keeps improving. None of this looks like a company hitting a wall. It looks like a company being throttled by how fast the backend can ship components. The optical push is the other half that the market's pricing in. Hyperscalers are running into the physical limits of copper interconnects at these data rates. 800G, 1.6T, 3.2T — you need optics or you're not scaling the next wave of AI training and inference clusters. Credo's been moving aggressively into this with their own development and they just closed the DustPhotonics acquisition in late May. DustPhotonics brings silicon photonics technology for near-packaged and co-packaged optics. Credo's now positioned across the whole optical stack for the speeds that matter in the next 18 to 36 months. They're guiding for over $600 million in optical revenue in fiscal 2027. That's not a side bet — it's becoming a core growth driver. SpaceX getting into AI cloud is another tailwind. They've already signed Anthropic and Alphabet as customers. If they're building out infrastructure at the scale everyone expects, Credo's interconnects are on the buyer list. Supply constraints or not, the addressable market for high-speed connectivity is expanding faster than almost anyone modeled a year ago. The risk that spooked people today is Meta saying they might sell excess AI compute capacity. The bears immediately jumped on it as proof of overbuilding. I'm not buying the panic. Meta spent aggressively and may have overshot their own internal needs. That doesn't mean there's market-wide overcapacity or that enterprise AI demand is softening. It means Meta has spare capacity and sees a commercial opportunity. If anything, it validates that the infrastructure they built works and that external demand exists. Watch it, but it's not a structural red flag for someone like Credo. If real demand were collapsing, you'd see it in their backlog and guidance. You'd see price pressure and margin compression. You're seeing the opposite. The valuation argument is straightforward. Forward P/E of 43 sounds rich in a vacuum. Compared to a company growing revenue at 111% and earnings even faster with expanding margins, it's not. The forward PEG ratio sits at 0.98, which is below 1.0 and nearly 30% cheaper than the broader IT sector. The market's giving you triple-digit growth at a discount to the sector multiple when you adjust for that growth. If the supply constraints ease and Credo can actually deliver the revenue they're leaving on the table, earnings estimates move up and the P/E compresses from here. If the optical ramp plays out and they take real share in that multi-hundred-million-dollar opportunity, the growth duration extends and the multiple has room to expand, not contract. The risk is if something breaks in AI infrastructure spending or if competition in optical heats up faster than expected and margins crack. Right now neither is showing up in the numbers. Demand is strong, supply is the limit, pricing holds, and they're investing in the right areas. Credo tripled because the growth is real, the margin story is clean, and the positioning for the next leg — optical connectivity at hyperscale — is stronger than it was six months ago. The deceleration everyone's worried about is a feature, not a bug. It's what happens when you sell out of everything you can build. The stock's not expensive. It's just no longer obscenely cheap. That's what happens when the market figures out the story before you do. text:
Market Review 20260701
- Rotation
- Money is leaving: AI trades, ie semiconductors, photonics, memory, data centers, neoclouds, space names etc
- Money is entering: Biotech, healthcare, fintech, finance, cybersecurity, selected software
- The rotation has not changed much. I was cautiously optimistic that the market could do well from here but looks like the recent leaders and the broad market are not ready yet. 
- Megacaps like the MAG7 caught a bit of a bid but I will want to see more life returning, and hope this is not just a dead cat bounce for them. 
- The big news today would be $META saying they want to sell their excess compute capacity and create an AI Cloud business. I don't think that is so easy. META is mainly an advertising / communications company. Building an AI cloud is not just about repositioning chips. That triggered broad selling across the AI plays, especially the neoclouds as that is what they had pivoted to doing from bitcoin mining. This is the "EXCUSE" that the market is looking for to sell. It was already wide and loose as I have mentioned in several posts recently. 
- $MU breaking its 20SMA and going back below 1000 will not be a good look. Photonics had already lost their leadership a few weeks ago. If Memory also loose leadership then the entire AI play may have to take a longer pause. Semiconductors are kindda holding it but also on the verge of loosing it. 
- I have also noticed some of the "old dogs" coming back to life. These are mainly software and fintech. Talking about HOOD, SOFI, UPST, DUOL, LMND, FIGR etc. Will they really resurrect and start leading?
- All the best.

text:
Hypothetically if hyperscalers paused for too long others would eventually step in. But there’s little incentive for an extended pause. They’d only be motivated to potentially ease off in the near term without fully stepping away. I’m saying this in the context of their stocks facing pressure, but more importantly how much future demand is being priced into may infra plays (photonics, semicaps, etc.). Forward PEs look very expensive if growth slows even modestly.

That said, I’m less convinced others could meaningfully fill any real HS gap if a true air pocket materializes in 27/28. Sovereigns tend to copycat hyperscaler/frontier thinking rather than lead aggressively on their own. China will likely stay aggressive, but their more efficient models mean they won’t drive as much incremental compute demand on a relative basis. I wouldn’t count on neoclouds ramping up enough given their heavy debt loads from this cycle, and other private infra players are generally conservative by nature and won’t swing big to backfill. So while some substitution happens, I doubt they’ll be as aggressive as the HS have been.

This would open the door to a potential air pocket where caution starts outweighing fresh spend and proven demand. Again, this is just an asymmetric optic that isn’t priced in at all after this latest bull run - a possible near-term correction where the “indefinite demand > supply” narrative gets tested.

This is all speculative and is probably too early. The HS rhetoric has been nothing but aggressive so far. But any rally/relief valve in their stocks might be welcomed as a signal that they recognize the FCF strain and aren’t being reckless.

They’ve been able to afford this level of spending so far thanks to their balance sheets, and it has also helped motivate vendors to keep pushing supply. But for how much longer? Especially if they start needing to lever with debt before the next real demand wave hits?

The main missing piece of compute capacity right now seems tied to big agentic/agent workflows at enterprise scale. But even that might arrive slower than hoped (due to internal politics, red tape, other integration hurdles, learning curve). A lot of today’s visible demand is also hype driven/token maxxing - whether its enterprises testing things out or retail usage that may not translate into sustained high value workloads.

I do feel that memory isn’t in the same bucket as broader infra on a valuation basis. It’s hard to fathom we’re still in such good shape valuation-wise after this run. One thing I forgot to mention in my post is that it’s not only the multimodal needs and emerging use cases outpacing new efficiencies (around KV cache, quantization, etc.), but also the deeper architectural reality of the memory stack itself. Model weights/parameters and activations/intermediate tensors (and optimizer states/gradients for any training or fine-tuning) are often larger than KV cache needs in most workloads. And these layers can’t all be tackled with the same KV-specific compression or efficiency tricks.

I’m saying this in the context of their stocks facing pressure, but more importantly how much future demand is being priced into may infra plays (photonics, semicaps, etc.). Forward PEs look very expensive if growth slows even modestly. That said, I’m less convinced others could meaningfully fill any real HS gap if a true air pocket materializes in 27/28. Sovereigns tend to copycat hyperscaler/frontier thinking rather than lead aggressively on their own. China will likely stay aggressive, but their more efficient models mean they won’t drive as much incremental compute demand on a relative basis. I wouldn’t count on neoclouds ramping up enough given their heavy debt loads from this cycle, and other private infra players are generally conservative by nature and won’t swing big to backfill. So while some substitution happens, I doubt they’ll be as aggressive as the HS have been. This would open the door to a potential air pocket where caution starts outweighing fresh spend and proven demand. Especially if they start needing to lever with debt before the next real demand wave hits? The main missing piece of compute capacity right now seems tied to big agentic/agent workflows at enterprise scale. But even that might arrive slower than hoped (due to internal politics, red tape, other integration hurdles, learning curve). text:
Inside Kopin's Vision for the Future.

What role will optical interconnects, MicroLED technology, and U.S.-based manufacturing play in the next generation of technology and AI?

Kopin CEO, President, and Chairperson Michael Murray sat down with Martin Gagel of @RadiusResearch to discuss Kopin's strategy, the evolution of jointly developed Neural I/o™, and the opportunities emerging at the intersection of AI infrastructure and advanced photonics.

Watch here: https://t.co/izhFjwEshz

#KopinTech #ArtificialIntelligence #AIInfrastructure #MicroLED #NeuralIO #Investing @MiTknlg

text:
https://t.co/0ncKgB2wBv

This is exactly the kind of experiment more people should be paying attention to.

Scientists used water waves to make an otherwise “invisible” quantum-style effect visible. A vortex in a tank acted as an analogue to the magnetic-flux structure in the Aharonov–Bohm effect. The waves did not need to pass through the vortex core for the global wave pattern to change.

That is the key point.

A hidden topological structure altered the phase of the wave environment, and the result became visible as system-wide nodal lines. Even more interesting: as the vortex strength increased, the number of rotating nodal lines became quantized.

To me, the deeper lesson is not “water proves quantum mechanics.”

It is that organized wave environments can reveal invisible structure through global diagnostic patterns.

Topology became visible.

Phase became visible.

A local organizing feature produced a non-local wave signature.

That matters far beyond this one experiment.

For ZPF Technologies, this is close to the language we are trying to formalize: dynamically organized electromagnetic environments should not only be analyzed by field strength or energy density. They should also be studied through phase, topology, boundary conditions, mode accessibility, noise structure, sidebands, linewidths, and residuals after standard explanations are subtracted.

If water waves can reveal hidden topological structure through nodal patterns, then advanced electromagnetic systems deserve the same kind of diagnostic imagination.

The frontier is not just building stronger fields.

It is learning how organized environments leave organized observables.

That is where topology, phase, stochastic dynamics, quantum vacuum diagnostics, photonics, plasma systems, and future fusion research may begin to converge.
#Quantum #quantumphysics #QuantumEngineering

text:
2026 PORTFOLIO UPDATE  Performance Metrics 

June 2026     :    6.39%
Harris YTD     : 45.79% 
S&P500 YTD: 12.84%  

My portfolio currently has 9 stocks: $nbis, $sofi, $mu, $aaoi, $asts, $oust, $pltr, $nflx, $onds

Currently 6/9 stocks in green, lagging only $sofi (who else!), $aaoi and $nflx.

Month Recap: It wasn't an easy month. It had a lot of ups and downs, and I'm really surprised that we closed the month in green. At one point I was -9%, but with the huge pump the last few days of the month, we managed to close in profit.

I will write down some thoughts of mine per each holding.
$nbis: This is the stock I have high conviction on. That doesn't mean I didn't trade it. I sold and bought quite a lot of times to make some profits and to initiate other positions. I've held it for one year now.
$sofi: I hold it from last year. Still in red, but I managed to bring my average down to $18.7, so I'm pretty close to breaking even. I love their CEO (@anthonynoto, send me a hat) as I believe he makes an extremely good job. The company is progressing really well, and the stock price will follow eventually.
$mu: I used to have SK Hynix, and I switched to have exposure to US markets. That's all. Same philosophy. Memory is in deep need.
$aaoi: A big player in photonics. All sector had a bad month. It will recover (I'm -14%), I'm not afraid.
$asts: I started buying when $spcx launched all the way to the bottom. I'm in green, I have a good average, and I believe a lot. Nothing more to say.
$oust: You see what happened last days with Physical AI and robots. Initially I bought in Aprin around $23 and sold at $36 for quick profit. Now I'm long here. Huge profit atm, I expect a pullback to take a breather.
$pltr & $nflx: I wanted exposure to both companies and I was looking for the opportunity. When $pltr dropped below 200week ema, I bought it and I have averaged. Netflix (last stock in red) gives me more opportunities for DCAing.
$onds: It was on my radar. I have issues with their CEO and his tactics (dilution and pumping), but at $7.9 it was a bargain and there was a huge disconnect between the company and the stock price. It's not a long-term position. When it reaches $10-$12, I planned to trim it.

Thanks for staying up to the end! If you enjoyed it, drop a 👋 emoji to see who read it.

Consider following me in order to be together in that journey. Today I reached 100k portfolio, so that's a huge milestone to celebrate!

$sofi, $mu, $aaoi, $asts, $oust, $pltr, $nflx, $onds Currently 6/9 stocks in green, lagging only $sofi (who else!), $aaoi and $nflx. Month Recap: It wasn't an easy month. It had a lot of ups and downs, and I'm really surprised that we closed the month in green. At one point I was -9%, but with the huge pump the last few days of the month, we managed to close in profit. I will write down some thoughts of mine per each holding. $nbis: This is the stock I have high conviction on. That doesn't mean I didn't trade it. I sold and bought quite a lot of times to make some profits and to initiate other positions. I've held it for one year now. $sofi: I hold it from last year. Still in red, but I managed to bring my average down to $18.7, so I'm pretty close to breaking even. I love their CEO (@anthonynoto, send me a hat) as I believe he makes an extremely good job. The company is progressing really well, and the stock price will follow eventually. $mu: I used to have SK Hynix, and I switched to have exposure to US markets. That's all. Same philosophy. Memory is in deep need. $aaoi: A big player in photonics. All sector had a bad month. It will recover (I'm -14%), I'm not afraid. $asts: I started buying when $spcx launched all the way to the bottom. I'm in green, I have a good average, and I believe a lot. Nothing more to say. $oust: You see what happened last days with Physical AI and robots. Initially I bought in Aprin around $23 and sold at $36 for quick profit. Now I'm long here. Huge profit atm, I expect a pullback to take a breather. $pltr & $nflx: I wanted exposure to both companies and I was looking for the opportunity. When $pltr dropped below 200week ema, I bought it and I have averaged. Netflix (last stock in red) gives me more opportunities for DCAing. $onds: It was on my radar. I have issues with their CEO and his tactics (dilution and pumping), but at $7.9 it was a bargain and there was a huge disconnect between the company and the stock price. It's not a long-term position. When it reaches $10-$12, I planned to trim it. Thanks for staying up to the end! If you enjoyed it, drop a 👋 emoji to see who read it. Consider following me in order to be together in that journey. text:
Half of the year is overwith. 

I have been fairly inactive over the last month as I spend time reflecting on the year, analyzing my holdings and seeing new opportunities for 2H. 

If I compartmentalize the year by months, I outperformed the majority in months 1-3 by having a defensive portfolio 25’ that was primary legacy manufacturers like $vrt $cat $gev. From April -> May I slightly underperformed, by not being able to pivot fast enough into emerging themes like memory and semis, only getting exposure to photonics through $aaoi. 

May -> present was OK, although my heavy positioning in $spcx ancillaries pulled performance down. Looking ahead I see no reason to 2H 26’ to be any different than what we have seen for the last few years. 

As I’m writing this I see Burry is now short my second largest holding caterpillar and I kindly ask him to accept my ballbag as I maintain bullish on the resurgence of heavy machinery and manufacturing names amidst an extremely pro America/infrastructure administration.

text:
Here's the post:

If I could only watch four layers in optical interconnect, ranked by bottleneck severity:
$COHR $LITE $FN $ANET

Nvidia just spent $6 billion locking down optical supply between March and June, and the market still thinks this is about transceivers.

The real story is InP substrates and laser chips — the stuff 20 layers upstream from what most people are tracking. Here's how I'd rank the supply chain by choke point plus earnings visibility over the next 12-24 months.

1. InP materials and epitaxy — the actual valve on compute scale. $COHR and $LITE aren't just CPO plays. They control high-purity indium phosphide epitaxy and high-speed laser IP that physically cannot be replaced in 800G/1.6T modules. Intel's CEO said it outright: InP is the non-negotiable material for solving chip-to-chip bandwidth. Nvidia locked each of them with separate $2 billion commitments and long-term offtake agreements. The Street prices them as module vendors. They're actually material oligopolists with compounding pricing power through every speed iteration.

2. CPO laser engines — the leverage on every GPU watt. $LITE and $COHR again, but now wearing their co-packaged optics hat. CPO moves the photonics inside the package, right next to the switch ASIC or GPU. That requires advanced packaging plus high-power lasers that don't cook themselves. Supply is sewn up. Goldman just put the scale-up interconnect TAM at $154 billion, and most of that growth is inside the rack and inside the super-pod, not between data centers. CPO sits in the middle of that build-out. Earnings inflection is 2026-2028 when hyperscalers move from pilots to volume. Risk is simple: if Nvidia capex wobbles, these names will gap hard because the trade is already crowded.

3. Module assembly and high-mix manufacturing — where cash flow actually prints. $FN is the TSMC of optical: low-drama contract manufacturing for every major module and CPO vendor. Margins are thinner than the chip guys, but revenue visibility is the cleanest in the whole chain because hyperscale deployment schedules are locked 18 months out. TrendForce just lifted 2025-2026 AI transceiver revenue from $16.5 billion to $26 billion, up 57% year-over-year. FN captures that with zero demand risk and no inventory. It's the only name here where you can model the next eight quarters with a straight face.

4. Switch and networking integration — the layer everyone already owns. $ANET is the consensus optical AI trade. Data center switching tied to optical back-end is a known quantity. Multiples reflect it. I'd rather own the three above because the market hasn't priced the material and assembly bottleneck the same way it's priced the switch attach rate.

The non-consensus bit: A-share money has been rotating from NAND and legacy semis into InP names for two quarters, but U.S. allocators still think optical is a 2025 story. It's a 2026-2028 margin story, and the earnings gap between upstream material plays and downstream module integrators is about to widen hard. I'm overweight the top two on that list until CPO production data forces multiple expansion.

Computational Ghost Imaging reconstructs high-resolution images using just a single-pixel detector—opening new possibilities for X-ray imaging, biomedical research, and beyond.

#Photonics #Optics #Imaging #XRay #Innovation

text:
This 🎯roadmap🎯 for data-center interconnect speeds is also, in many ways, a roadmap for #siliconphotonics.  As data rates move higher, the industry will need more integrated optical solutions, including near-package optics and co-packaged optics. That is where #siliconphotonics becomes increasingly relevant.
I adapted this roadmap based on analysis from an investment bank, which states that it is built on company disclosures as well as its own research.
It is interesting to observe how the upgrade cycles are overlapping. According to this analysis 400G is already moving into decline, 800G is becoming the mainstream workhorse, 1.6T is entering mass-volume territory, and 3.2T is already visible before 1.6T has fully settled. Furthermore, 800G may remain mainstream for only around six quarters. That is shorter than the typical 2–4 year cadence often expected for a mainstream optical transceiver generation. This points to a compressed innovation cadence for the industry. Qualification, yield learning, supply-chain readiness, thermal design, packaging, DSP, and test infrastructure all need to mature faster than before. Only the 💪fittest can survive this race. My view is that integrated photonics is entering a sustained innovation cycle, driven by the bandwidth, power, density, and packaging pressures of AI data-center infrastructure. 
🚀 #siliconphotonics

text: 6G's whole pitch rides on terahertz, and terahertz is brutal to actually build antennas for. This new compact "topological" antenna (in Nature Photonics) handles those info-dense THz signals — exactly the unglamorous breakthrough that makes the flashy 6G demos possible. @NaturePhotonics @jeffkagan https://t.co/zwoCLtnzoy
