Russia ITMO University: Optical Tractor Beam with Metasurfaces and United States: Boeing's Magnetic Tractor Beam Patent

#ufotwitter

It's fascinating milestone in the field of modern nanophotonics. Published in ACS Photonics in late 2018 by physicists from ITMO University, the research details how hyperbolic metasurfaces can be used to generate stable optical pulling forces—commonly referred to as an "optical tractor beam."

To understand how this functions, it helps to look at the mechanics of standard radiation pressure and contrast it with how these specialized, engineered surfaces flip that mechanic on its head.

The Problem with Normal Light

Under ordinary conditions, when a laser beam strikes a small dielectric particle, the photons transfer their linear momentum to the object. This creates a standard scattering or radiation pressure that pushes the particle away from the light source, along the direction of propagation.

Creating a "tractor beam" requires generating a negative optical force (a pulling force) that acts against the global momentum of the incoming light, drawing the particle backward toward the source.

How Hyperbolic Metasurfaces Change the Mechanics

A hyperbolic metasurface is an ultra-thin, artificially engineered material characterized by extreme anisotropy. This means its electromagnetic properties (specifically its permittivity, have different signs along different spatial axes—for example, acting like a metal  in one direction and like a dielectric in another.

When a small particle is placed close to this surface and illuminated by a standard unstructured light source (like a plane wave), the interaction relies on three core phenomena:

High Density of States & Asymmetric Scattering: The hyperbolic nature of the substrate opens up highly localized, high-density electromagnetic interaction channels (both surface plasmons and volumetric waves within the substrate). The presence of the particle scatterer near the interface selectively excites these asymmetrical near-field modes.

Momentum Conservation and Recoil: Because the substrate allows light to scatter preferentially into these highly confined, high-momentum modes directed forward along or into the surface, the law of conservation of linear momentum requires a balance. To compensate for the forward momentum of the tailored scattering channels, the particle experiences a net mechanical recoil in the opposite direction.

Broadband Pulling: Unlike older optical trapping configurations that require complex, actively modulated phase holograms or spatial light modulators (SLMs) to sculpt a specific beam geometry (like a solenoid or Bessel beam), this model utilizes the intrinsic dispersion of the substrate itself. This allows for stable optical pulling forces over a significantly broader spectral range using simpler incident light fields.

Why This Mattered

Prior to this approach, pulling particles with light required highly complex, bulky optical setups that restricted spatial movement and demanded high laser power, which could damage biological samples or nanoscale structures. By shifting the engineering challenge from the light source to the material environment (the metasurface), this framework opened up new avenues for nanoscale manipulation, chip-based sorting of cells or macromolecules, and nanoscale fluidic self-organization.

United States: Boeing's Magnetic Tractor Beam Patent

This is an interesting find. You’re looking at US Patent US6634603B2, titled "Magnetic dipole tractor beam control system", filed by The Boeing Company back in 2001 and granted in 2003.

While the phrase "tractor beam" instantly evokes sci-fi, the actual engineering framework here is grounded in classic electromagnetic field manipulation. Here is a breakdown of what this patent actually describes, how it works, and its intended application.

The Core Concept: How It Works

Unlike fictional tractor beams that use exotic energy to pull objects across massive distances, this system relies on the predictable physics of magnetic dipole interactions.

If you have two magnetic dipoles (which can be generated using electromagnets or superconducting loops), they exert a force and a torque on one another based on their relative orientation, distance, and field strength.

The patent describes a system designed to:

Attract or Repel: Manipulate the magnetic fields to pull a target object closer or push it away.

Control Orientation: Adjust the torque to precisely rotate, stabilize, or align the target object in three-dimensional space without physical contact.

Dynamic Feedback: Continuously measure the position and orientation of the target, feeding that data into a control processor to adjust the magnetic fields in real-time, preventing the target from drifting or snapping violently out of position.

The Intended Application

In space, docking two multi-ton vehicles (like a shuttle or capsule connecting to a space station) is incredibly risky. Mechanical docking mechanisms require physical impact, which can transmit dangerous kinetic energy, cause structural fatigue, or send both craft into an uncontrolled tumble if misaligned.

The "Tractor Beam" Solution:

By using this magnetic dipole control system, a chaser spacecraft could:

Approach a passive target satellite or station module.

Activate the controlled magnetic fields from a safe distance.

Gently capture, damp out any residual spin (tumble) of the target, and guide it into a precise mechanical lock without ever touching it until the final capture.

It's a highly sophisticated, non-contact magnetic tethering and dampening system—essentially a high-tech "soft-capture" mechanism for orbital logistics.

Kepler Aerospace is a Texas-based company positioning itself as an "Exotic Propulsion and Exotic Energy Company." Their stated focus spans several advanced and unconventional areas, including:

Propulsion & Energy: They claim to work on proprietary technologies such as exotic electromagnetic propulsion, compact mobile fusion, and "gravity modification" or "gravitational shielding."

Terrestrial Applications: Beyond space flight, they are developing systems intended to process fossil fuels into cleaner-burning aviation and diesel fuels.

Divisions: The company operates through several specialized units, including Manufacturing, Astrodynamics, Electronics, Geophysics, and a Military division focused on security technologies.

The company leadership includes Dr. John Brandenburg as Chief Science Officer. Their public-facing presence highlights an interest in both commercial and military applications, with some activities centered on aerospace training and development in the West Texas region.

Kepler Aerospace positions itself at the intersection of conventional aerospace manufacturing and high-risk, unconventional physics. Here are the key takeaways regarding their technical focus and mission:

Core Technical Mandate: The company explicitly identifies as an "Exotic Propulsion and Exotic Energy Company." They state they are pursuing technologies that move beyond standard aerospace limitations, specifically citing compact mobile fusion, exotic electromagnetic propulsion, and gravity modification/shielding.

Dual-Use Strategic Focus: Their business model is divided into two distinct lanes:

Space/Defense: Leveraging advanced, potentially paradigm-shifting physics for astrodynamics and military security applications.

Terrestrial Energy: Developing processes to convert fossil fuels into cleaner-burning aviation and diesel fuels, positioning this as a key sustainability and emissions-reduction product.

Scientific Leadership: Their technical direction is influenced by Dr. John Brandenburg, who serves as Chief Science Officer. Brandenburg is known for unconventional hypotheses, including his work suggesting evidence of large-scale thermonuclear explosions on Mars in the deep past.

Operational Footprint: They maintain a suite of divisions—Manufacturing, Astrodynamics, Electronics, Geophysics, and Military—and emphasize operations out of the West Texas region, often engaging in aerospace training and regional development initiatives.

Business Positioning: While they emphasize "commercially viable" development, their public profile leans heavily on high-concept research. They maintain a firm stance against critical outside scrutiny, as evidenced by recent public releases addressing and dismissing online allegations regarding the company.

Advisory Board
The company utilizes an advisory board consisting of figures associated with alternative physics, advanced engineering, and legacy defense programs:

Dr. Robert M. L Baker, Jr.: A foundational figure in modern astrodynamics who is heavily involved in high-frequency gravitational wave research.

Herman “David” Froning: An aerospace engineer noted for publishing extensive theoretical work on field propulsion, vacuum engineering, and laser-driven space flight.

John Cole & Dr. Frank Mead: Both have histories connected to advanced or breakthrough propulsion concepts within institutional or military frameworks.

Alexander Karimov: Kepler Aerospace Advisor

Alexander Karimov is an academic and researcher operating at the high end of Russian experimental and theoretical physics. His credentials include:

Doctor of Science (Physics and Mathematics, 1999): Obtained from the prestigious Moscow Engineering Physical Institute (MEPhI), a premier Soviet/Russian institution tightly bound to fundamental nuclear research and advanced military-industrial technology. He also completed his Ph.D. there in 1994.

Professor at MEPhI: Specifically within the Department of Electrophysical Installations, which deals directly with high-energy systems, particle accelerators, and plasma physics.

Leading Researcher at JIHT RAS: Since 1996, he has been a principal researcher at the Joint Institute for High Temperatures of the Russian Academy of Sciences (Division for Magneto-plasma aerodynamics and MHD energy conversion).

Karimov’s background bridges aggressive aerospace engineering concepts with fundamental energy physics. The bio highlights several highly specialized pillars:

Magnetohydrodynamic (MHD) Energy Conversion: This involves generating electrical power directly from moving plasma or high-velocity ionized gases without moving mechanical parts. In aerospace, MHD is heavily researched for advanced propulsion, hypersonic flow control (reducing drag or thermal loads on re-entry vehicles), and power extraction.

Magneto-plasma Aerodynamics: The study of how magnetic fields interact with ionized air flows (plasmas) around a vehicle. This has direct implications for stealth (plasma stealth fields), hyper-velocity flight, and atmospheric propulsion.

Laser, Beam, and Plasma Physics: Core disciplines involving high-energy directional beams and the manipulation of plasma states.

Hydrodynamic Coherent Structures: The text notes his specific work on the "Role of Initial Conditions in the Formation of Coherent Structures in Hydrodynamic Systems," an area of fluid mechanics critical to understanding how stable vortexes, plasmas, or fluid structures form and persist.

His Role at Kepler Aerospace

According to the company profile, Karimov serves as an Advisor. His specific mandate within the company is to guide the team on:

Plasma Products & Plasma Engineering: Utilizing his decades of research at the Russian Academy of Sciences to help design systems that manipulate or generate plasma fields.

The Physical AI Thematic is moving really fast...
$OUST $AMBA $AEVA $SYNA $CGNX $VPG

Over the last few weeks, I put out two layer reports

> A deep dive into the perception layer
> A deep dive into the edge compute layer

The top 3 companies from each layer I presented:

> $AMBA $OUST $CGNX 
> $SYNA $CEVA $LSCC

So the way it works is 3 steps:

1 - I present the 'layer' as an overview like why it's important, how dollars are captured, the durability etc.

2 - Within this report I present the top 3 companies I believe are well positioned. A quick thesis / overview

3 - Once I decide upon the top candidates, I then do a full deep dive into every aspect of the company, along with the weightings I am interested in and the financial modeling around them

While this is a very exciting time for this theme, we are still very early. There is no rush on my end for positioning or research. 

I started covering Physical AI ~6 weeks ago and along with photonics, these are the two themes I am researching and covering in depth. 

If we can really identify the strongest companies, we can position with a multi-year mindset

I still have 7 more layers to present and lots of positions I still need to take

Join us if you are interested! 

https://t.co/Xv5BKKCwOR

text:
🚨 A new AI infrastructure bottleneck just got its own ETF — backed by SemiAnalysis

Introducing $LAZR — the Tema Photonics & Optical ETF, launched in direct partnership with @SemiAnalysis, the leading independent research shop in semis & AI infra.

$LAZR is pure-play exposure to the optics layer moving data between chips, servers & data centers at AI scale — the second critical bottleneck after memory (its sister fund $DISK)

Top 10 holdings:

Lumentum ( $LITE) 14.6% — lasers/transceivers powering high-speed data center links

Cash  holdings 11.4%

Aixtron 10.5% — German MOCVD deposition equipment for compound semis

$AXTI AXT Inc 9.7% — substrates underpinning optical & power devices

$AAOI Applied Optoelectronics 5.8%

Furukawa Electric 5.2%

$TSEM Tower Semiconductor 4.1%

Landmark Optoelectronics ~3.9%

$AEHR Aehr Test Systems 3.5%

Yangtze Optical Fibre & Cable 3.4%

Why it matters?? 
📊Goldman projects the AI data-movement/photonics market to grow 9x between 2026-2028. 
📈As bandwidth needs outrun copper’s physics, silicon photonics stops being a niche play and becomes core infrastructure.

$PLTR – beweist immer deutlicher, wie man mit KI-gestützter Daten-Analyse in der echten Wirtschaft massiv Geld verdient.

Setzt ihr im 2. Halbjahr nur auf die bekannten Tech-Giganten oder habt ihr auch solche Small-Cap-Wetten im Depot?

text:
Daily Buzzberg Alpha — July 3

The tape sold AI/semis like Meta capex = overcapacity. The best sources read the same data as proof of scarcity.

1. The neocloud selloff looks like a misread. SemiAnalysis flipped the bear narrative: Meta isn't cutting AI capex — it's turning compute into a business. 5GW+ already contracted in H1, and 2027 capex could come in above expectations. Short-term compute reportedly trades ~4x long-term neocloud rates — that's not what surplus looks like. Read-through: $NBIS $CRWV $IREN $CIFR. The outlier: $ORCL, flagged for weak compute monetization vs peers.

2. Memory upgraded from "shortage" to pricing power. The HBM chatter got specific: 2027 contract talk now includes 2–2.5x price increases vs broker estimates. CNBC: the Apple AI iPhone cycle could lift memory demand ~50%, with Apple hunting supply even from Chinese makers. Note — the same Apple-China headline traded as a bear signal yesterday; today's read is demand outrunning supply. Macro counterpoint: pricing near peak, trade crowded. $MU  $SNDK , Samsung and SK Hynix in Seoul.

3. Korea is a market-structure trade now, not just a memory proxy. Local channels near-unanimous: the selloff was technical — 2x leveraged products, foreign flows, pension rebalancing — not broken fundamentals. Earnings + HBM LTAs + cheap valuation on one side, index-shock sensitivity to every AI rumor on the other. $EWY.

4. Photonics/packaging — best undercovered signal of the day. From SemiAnalysis' ECTC notes: Marvell custom HBM cuts host ASIC area ~60%, TSMC direct-to-silicon cooling handles >5kW, optical interconnects becoming the core scaling path. Supply-chain angle: $LITE demand reportedly 30%+ ahead of supply, $COHR locking wafer supply via $AXTI, $CIEN as the cleaner transport play. Also $MRVL $IQE $TSM $INTC.

5. Physical AI: quieter, higher quality. BlueCrest disclosed a 5.6% stake in $CCXI  (the Agility Robotics proxy). $ONDS expanded its counter-drone TAM from federal to state/local. $BOT as the public basket. $OUST cooling off on fresh mentions.

The non-consensus read: if compute is still scarce and Meta is monetizing it, yesterday's selloff was positioning and liquidity — not a fundamental break.

text:
WHILE EVERYONE PANICS OVER CHIP STOCKS, THE REAL AI INFRASTRUCTURE PLAY IS QUIETLY SELLING OFF TOO.

$AMKR Amkor Technology down 12.93%. Semiconductor packaging and testing, critical link between chip design and the end product.

$LITE Lumentum down 9.09%. Optical components and photonics for datacenters and telecom, key infrastructure for AI networking.

$VRT Vertiv down 3.5%. Power and cooling infrastructure for data centers, the backbone that keeps AI compute running.

$MPWR Monolithic Power Systems down 3.27%. Power management chips used across AI hardware.

Different layers of the same AI buildout story, all getting hit together.

DYOR.

text:
Posted by Antithesis Capital on LinkedIn in February 2026: 

Capitalizing on the Semiconductor Demand Cycle on the OTC market

SCI Engineered Materials (SCIA) is a global supplier and manufacturer of advanced materials for Physical Vapor Deposition (PVD) thin film applications. SCIA is mainly focused on markets within the photonics industry. Their main product is Sputtering Targets which are specialized solid materials used in thin-film deposition processes called Sputtering for semiconductors, displays, optics, and coatings. 

How Sputtering Works:
- A sputtering target acts as the source material
- The target is bombarded with high-energy ions 
- These ions knock atoms or molecules loose from the target's surface
- The freed atoms travel through the vacuum and deposit onto a nearby surface (the substrate), forming an extremely thin, uniform film 

Demand for sputtering targets continues to increase as the Semiconductor boom accelerates. Sputtering targets are  essential for depositing thin-film layers (interconnects like copper, barriers like tantalum/tungsten, insulators) during wafer fabrication via physical vapor deposition.

SCIA trades on the OTC market with a market-cap of $26M. Meanwhile, their balance sheet consists of the following: 

Cash: $9.5M
A/R: $900K
Inventory: $3M 
Marketable Securities: $3.3M
Total Liabilities: $6.3M 

$4M of the $6.3M in Total Liabilities is Customer Deposits which can be better defined as Order Backlog and signals on-going demand for SCIA’s products. SCIA had $1.9M and $2.2M in Net Income in 2024 and 2023 respectively. They’ve made $1.2M in Net Income through 9/30/2025. 

SCIA’s closest NYSE/NASDAQ comp is Materion Corporation (MTRN). Materion is an integrated producer of high-performance advanced engineered materials used in a variety of electrical, thermal, and structural applications. 

MRTN trades at an above market multiple with a PE of 163. In addition, they were recently upgraded to Overweight by KeyBank. According to the report, data points suggest MTRN is well positioned to capitalize on a global semiconductor consumable demand cycle which could feature higher aerospace and defense budgets, expanding clean energy businesses, and opportunities for margin expansion. 

We agree with KeyBank’s thought process and think SCI Engineered Materials will have a chance to capitalize on this same demand cycle. In our opinion, SCIA is a much more attractive way to play this demand cycle thesis due their rock solid balance sheet and reasonable PE multiple (16x). 

We are not the only ones who think this. Management, on 11/17/25, announced a $1 million share repurchase authorization. Clearly, they see the same value. 

SCIA is set to report FY25 earnings this week.

"id": "Tm90ZVR3ZWV0OjIwNzMxMjAzODQwNTMwODQxNjA=", "text": "Posted by Antithesis Capital on LinkedIn in February 2026: Capitalizing on the Semiconductor Demand Cycle on the OTC market SCI Engineered Materials (SCIA) is a global supplier and manufacturer of advanced materials for Physical Vapor Deposition (PVD) thin film applications. SCIA is mainly focused on markets within the photonics industry. Their main product is Sputtering Targets which are specialized solid materials used in thin-film deposition processes called Sputtering for semiconductors, displays, optics, and coatings. How Sputtering Works: - A sputtering target acts as the source material - The target is bombarded with high-energy ions - These ions knock atoms or molecules loose from the target's surface - The freed atoms travel through the vacuum and deposit onto a nearby surface (the substrate), forming an extremely thin, uniform film Demand for sputtering targets continues to increase as the Semiconductor boom accelerates. Sputtering targets are essential for depositing thin-film layers (interconnects like copper, barriers like tantalum/tungsten, insulators) during wafer fabrication via physical vapor deposition. SCIA trades on the OTC market with a market-cap of $26M. Meanwhile, their balance sheet consists of the following: Cash: $9.5M A/R: $900K Inventory: $3M Marketable Securities: $3.3M Total Liabilities: $6.3M $4M of the $6.3M in Total Liabilities is Customer Deposits which can be better defined as Order Backlog and signals on-going demand for SCIA’s products. SCIA had $1.9M and $2.2M in Net Income in 2024 and 2023 respectively. They’ve made $1.2M in Net Income through 9/30/2025. SCIA’s closest NYSE/NASDAQ comp is Materion Corporation (MTRN). Materion is an integrated producer of high-performance advanced engineered materials used in a variety of electrical, thermal, and structural applications. MRTN trades at an above market multiple with a PE of 163. In addition, they were recently upgraded to Overweight by KeyBank. According to the report, data points suggest MTRN is well positioned to capitalize on a global semiconductor consumable demand cycle which could feature higher aerospace and defense budgets, expanding clean energy businesses, and opportunities for margin expansion. We agree with KeyBank’s thought process and think SCI Engineered Materials will have a chance to capitalize on this same demand cycle. In our opinion, SCIA is a much more attractive way to play this demand cycle thesis due their rock solid balance sheet and reasonable PE multiple (16x). We are not the only ones who think this. Management, on 11/17/25, announced a $1 million share repurchase authorization. Clearly, they see the same value. SCIA is set to report FY25 earnings this week."}}}, "rest_id": "2073120384283807751", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "29", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
The firm whose bearish CPO paper helped tank optical stocks just launched a photonics ETF and it's holding $SIVE.

Walk through what actually happened:

→ SemiAnalysis published a scathing piece on CPO delays and optical valuations. Optical names sold off 40-60%.

→ On June 30, SemiAnalysis co-launched the Tema Photonics & Optical ETF (NYSE: LAZR) — the first institutionally managed photonics ETF — in partnership with Tema ETFs.

→ As of July 1, that ETF holds 26,688 shares of $SIVE, bought around 48 SEK.

So the same research shop that helped drive the sector down is now buying into it through an institutional product — a 24-name concentrated fund built specifically for photonics and optical exposure.

I'm not going to call that anything it isn't. What I'll say is this:

When you get to publish the bearish note AND launch the fund that buys the dip, that's the most enviable seat in the market. And the name they put in the fund — out of every photonics company on Earth, in a concentrated 24-holding portfolio — was $SIVE.

An actively managed, SemiAnalysis-backed, institutional photonics fund looked at the entire optical universe and decided Sivers belonged in the 24.

That's not retail hype. That's a research-driven institutional desk putting $SIVE in the same basket as Lumentum, Coherent, and AXT.

The company keeps ending up exactly where the smartest money in the sector is looking.

$SIVE $LAZR

text:
Here's the takeaway that actually matters: $AAOI, $LITE, $COHR, $CIEN, $NOK are down 25-55% on sector weakness.

The photonics demand curve extends into 2027-2028 regardless of when CPO hits mass production. As clusters scale, copper hits a wall. Optical isn't optional. I'm buying the fear, not the narrative.

How far off their highs, and what you make just reclaiming them:

• $SIVEF $5.24 | down 53% | +115% to reclaim $11.25
• $AAOI $121 | down 48% | +93% to reclaim $234
• $CIEN $422 | down 34% | +51% to reclaim $638
• $LITE $725 | down 33% | +50% to reclaim $1,086
• $NOK $12 | down 31% | +45% to reclaim $17.45 (52w)
• $COHR $333 | down 24% | +32% to reclaim $440

And that's just to tag old highs.

text:
AXT เซ็นดีล 3 ปีกับ Coherent ขยายกำลังผลิตวัสดุชิปโฟโตนิกส์

AXT $AXTI เปิดเผยผ่านเอกสารยื่นต่อ SEC ว่าบริษัทในเครือ AXT-Tongmei ได้ทำข้อตกลงระยะเวลา 3 ปีร่วมกับ Coherent $COHR

ดีลนี้เกี่ยวข้องกับการพัฒนาและจัดส่ง substrate อินเดียมฟอสไฟด์ขนาด 6 นิ้ว ซึ่งเป็นวัสดุสำคัญในอุตสาหกรรม photonics หรือเทคโนโลยีการส่งข้อมูลด้วยแสง

ขยายกำลังผลิตในจีนเพื่อรองรับคำสั่งซื้อ
ภายใต้ข้อตกลงนี้ AXT จะขยายกำลังการผลิตที่โรงงานในกรุงปักกิ่ง ระหว่างปี 2026 ถึง 2028
โดยผลผลิตทั้งหมดจะถูกจัดสรรให้กับ Coherent ตามสัญญาที่กำหนดไว้

Coherent วางเงินล่วงหน้ากว่า 22 ล้านดอลลาร์
Coherent จะจ่ายเงินล่วงหน้าประมาณ 22.29 ล้านดอลลาร์สหรัฐ
เงินจำนวนนี้จะถูกนำไปใช้เป็นเครดิตสำหรับการซื้อสินค้าล็อตในอนาคต

อย่างไรก็ตาม เอกสารระบุเงื่อนไขสำคัญว่า
หาก Coherent ใช้คำสั่งซื้อตามขั้นต่ำไม่ครบ เงินล่วงหน้าอาจ “ไม่สามารถขอคืนได้”
แต่หาก Coherent เลือกยกเลิกดีลก่อนโดยไม่ผิดเงื่อนไข จะสามารถขอคืนเงินได้

ในทางกลับกัน
ถ้า AXT ส่งมอบกำลังผลิตไม่ได้ตามที่ตกลงนานเกิน 6 เดือน
Coherent มีสิทธิ์ยกเลิกสัญญาและขอคืนเงินที่ยังไม่ได้ใช้

text:
🚀  $SIVEF  🚀 

The ABC retracement in Wave C got extended but I believe we are close to a reversal.
I’m very bullish on this Ai core play who’s a provider of laser and packaged optics.

Daily chart 👇
Stopped on my line !
RSI low but not oversold, because the underlying trend strength is so strong. 
We already reach the max fib retracement 61.8%.
4H chart 👇
Bottom signal didn’t confirm and no bullish divergence on the RSI yet.
I will wait for a reversal sign to add.

NEWS
- Dilution announced on July 1st
- The company is actively preparing for a U.S. Nasdaq listing which could be a big catalyst for the next wave.

After looking at the chart closer I changed my wave count :
I now think that this correction is large cycle II with large wave III coming soon with targets $14-$16.50
Extended $19.50-$21.50

text:
かつて協働研究を行なっていた文化人類学者の宮永國子氏は、ワークショップに一時参加していた古河電工のベテランエンジニアの方を、「彼は天才です。」と言っていたのを思い出した。 🌹Grok『古河電工（古河電気工業）の基幹的イノベーションテクノロジーは、創業以来一貫して「メタル（Metals）」「ポリマー（Polymers）」「フォトニクス（Photonics）」「高周波（High Frequency）」の4つのコア技術を軸としています。これらは電線製造技術から自然に派生・進化したもので、現在も同社の全事業の基盤となっています。
4つのコア技術の概要
メタル: 銅精錬・金属加工技術から発展。導電材、超電導線材、カーボンナノチューブ、高機能銅箔・条など。
ポリマー: 絶縁材・機能樹脂の開発技術。自動車部品、放熱材、半導体製造用テープ、機能性フィルムなど。
フォトニクス: 光ファイバー・光デバイス・レーザー技術。情報通信の基幹を担い、近年は加工・医療分野にも拡大。
高周波: RF・高周波電子技術。無線通信部品、自動車電子部品、高速基板材料など。
これらを単独で深化させるだけでなく、融合させて社会課題解決型の新事業を創出している点が現在の特徴です。
過去の基幹的イノベーション（歴史的マイルストーン）
古河電工のイノベーションは、電線・電力インフラから情報通信・先進材料へという大きなシフトが特徴です。
1884年創業〜戦前: 足尾銅山由来の銅精錬から電線製造へ。日本初の海底ケーブル（1915年）や無装荷海底ケーブル（1937年、世界初）など、メタル＋ポリマーの組み合わせで社会インフラを支えました。
1974年: 世界初の光ファイバーケーブルフィールド試験に成功。これがフォトニクス事業の本格的な起点となりました。
1983年: 世界初の実用光ファイバ複合OFケーブルの商業化。
2001年: 米Lucent Technologiesの光ファイバ部門（OFS）買収。グローバル規模でフォトニクス技術・特許を強化。
2000年代〜2010年代: 超電導線材の開発が加速。CERN（欧州合同原子核研究機構）から特別賞受賞（2003年、LHC用）。2016年には希土類系超電導線材による永久電流技術を開発（MRIなど医療応用）。
2015年: 世界トップクラスの導電率を持つカーボンナノチューブの開発成功（メタル分野の先進材料イノベーション）。
この時期は「銅の会社から光の会社へ」の転換期であり、4コア技術の枠組みが明確に確立されました。
現在の基幹的イノベーションと応用
現在は4コア技術の深化＋融合＋デジタル化がキーワードです。古河電工グループVision 2030では、「情報・エネルギー・モビリティを組み合わせた次世代インフラソリューション」を目指しています。
1. フォトニクス分野の最先端
超多心・細径光ファイバケーブル: 2018年に世界最高水準のコア密度を実現した6912心細径超多心ケーブルを開発。以降も進化を続け、2026年3月には13824心（従来比約2倍容量）の量産を開始（AIデータセンター向け）。データ爆発（生成AI、5G/6G）に対応する基幹技術です。
光デバイス: 狭線幅・高出力マイクロITLA（2017年、100Gbps超光コヒーレント通信用）。
レーザー加工: Blue-IRハイブリッドレーザー「BRACE™」（2020年発売、日亜化学工業との協業）。青色レーザーと近赤外ファイバレーザーを組み合わせ、銅のスパッタレス高品質溶接を実現。EVバッテリーや電子部品製造に不可欠で、近年は5kW級青色レーザーモジュールまで進化。
2. メタル＋先進材料分野
超電導技術: 高温超電導集合導体の研究開発（NEDOプロジェクト）。浮体式洋上風力発電向け超高圧ダイナミック海底ケーブル（2019年受注）。核融合エネルギーや産業用大型電磁石への応用も進行中（2026年現在、英国Tokamak Energyとの協業も発表）。
軽量・高導電材料: カーボンナノチューブのワイヤ応用など。
3. ポリマー・高周波の応用
自動車電装システム（ワイヤハーネス、ステアリングロールコネクタ、先進安全センサー）。
放熱・冷却部品、半導体製造用テープ、高周波基板用銅箔など。EV・自動運転・5Gインフラを支えています。
4. 横断的イノベーション（特に重要）
DX（デジタルトランスフォーメーション）: 「デジタルトランスフォーメーション＆イノベーションセンター（DXIC）」を中核に、AI/IoTを製造現場に本格適用。SAP S/4HANA基幹システム刷新やデータ駆動型ものづくりを推進。
オープンイノベーション: 「Fun Lab®」をプラットフォームに、NEDOプロジェクト（金属積層造形へのフォトニクス活用など）やスタートアップ連携を積極化。
新分野展開: ライフサイエンス（光プローブやレーザー医療機器、蛍光シリカナノ粒子を用いた高感度検査キット）、インフラ維持管理、エアロスペースなどへ4コア技術を応用。』

text:
The AI Brain – 10 Infrastructure Layers related to neuroscience 
🧠 

1. Power & Electricity ⚡️ 
Neuroscience: Brainstem + Heart & Blood Vessels
AI infra: Power plants, electrical grids, substations
- This is the “blood” that keeps everything alive. AI needs huge amounts of electricity. No power = brain is dead

2. Cooling Systems 🧊 
Neuroscience: Body temperature control + Blood cooling
AI infra: Liquid cooling, big air conditioners, chillers
- AI chips get extremely hot, similar to running a marathon. Cooling stops the brain from overheating and breaking.

3. Datacenter Buildings 🏗️
Neuroscience: Skull + Outer Cerebral Cortex
AI infra: Huge warehouses full of server racks
- These are the “skull and brain tissue.” Big empty buildings that will later be filled. 

4. Compute Chips (GPUs & Accelerators) 💾
Neuroscience: Billions of Neurons (brain cells)
AI infra: The actual GPU chips and AI processors
- These are the individual “brain cells.” They do the heavy thinking and math for AI.

5. High-Speed Connections 🔌
Neuroscience: White Matter (nerve highways) + Corpus Callosum
AI infra: Fiber optic cables, networking switches, photonics
- Super-fast roads that let different parts of the brain talk to each other instantly.

6. Data Storage 💽
Anatomy: Hippocampus (memory center)
AI infra: Hard drives, SSDs, cloud storage systems
- This is where the brain keeps all its memories and knowledge safe for a long time.

7. Cloud & Neocloud Platforms 🧠☁️
Neuroscience: Neocortex (higher thinking areas)
AI infra: Companies like Nebius, CoreWeave, Crusoe, Lambda that rent out GPUs
- The “managers” that organize all the hardware and let people use the brain without owning it.

8. AI Software Frameworks 👨🏼‍💻
Neuroscience: Association areas (connecting ideas)
AI infra: Tools like PyTorch, Kubernetes, TensorFlow
- The easy-to-use “instruction manuals” that help people build and run AI on the hardware.

9. AI Models 🤓
Neuroscience: Prefrontal Cortex (planning & intelligence)
AI infra: Big models like GPT, Claude, Grok, Anthropic, etc.
- This is the actual “brain power” – the part that understands language, writes, codes, and reasons.

10. AI Agents & Robotics 🤖 
Neuroscience: Motor Cortex + Hands & Body (Peripheral Nervous System)
AI infra: AI agents, robots, self-driving cars, smart apps that take action
- This is the “body and hands.” It turns thinking into real-world actions.

Cooling Systems 🧊 Neuroscience: Body temperature control + Blood cooling AI infra: Liquid cooling, big air conditioners, chillers - AI chips get extremely hot, similar to running a marathon. Cooling stops the brain from overheating and breaking. 3. Datacenter Buildings 🏗️ Neuroscience: Skull + Outer Cerebral Cortex AI infra: Huge warehouses full of server racks - These are the “skull and brain tissue.” Big empty buildings that will later be filled. 4. Compute Chips (GPUs & Accelerators) 💾 Neuroscience: Billions of Neurons (brain cells) AI infra: The actual GPU chips and AI processors - These are the individual “brain cells.” They do the heavy thinking and math for AI. 5. High-Speed Connections 🔌 Neuroscience: White Matter (nerve highways) + Corpus Callosum AI infra: Fiber optic cables, networking switches, photonics - Super-fast roads that let different parts of the brain talk to each other instantly. 6. Data Storage 💽 Anatomy: Hippocampus (memory center) AI infra: Hard drives, SSDs, cloud storage systems - This is where the brain keeps all its memories and knowledge safe for a long time. 7. Cloud & Neocloud Platforms 🧠☁️ Neuroscience: Neocortex (higher thinking areas) AI infra: Companies like Nebius, CoreWeave, Crusoe, Lambda that rent out GPUs - The “managers” that organize all the hardware and let people use the brain without owning it. 8. AI Software Frameworks 👨🏼‍💻 Neuroscience: Association areas (connecting ideas) AI infra: Tools like PyTorch, Kubernetes, TensorFlow - The easy-to-use “instruction manuals” that help people build and run AI on the hardware. 9. AI Models 🤓 Neuroscience: Prefrontal Cortex (planning & intelligence) AI infra: Big models like GPT, Claude, Grok, Anthropic, etc. - This is the actual “brain power” – the part that understands language, writes, codes, and reasons. 10. AI Agents & Robotics 🤖 Neuroscience: Motor Cortex + Hands & Body (Peripheral Nervous System) AI infra: AI agents, robots, self-driving cars, smart apps that take action - This is the “body and hands.” It turns thinking into real-world actions."}}}, "rest_id": "2073497705662628263", "source": "Twitter for iPad", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "1139", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
$MRVL is becoming one of the most important names in the AI infrastructure buildout.

Nvidia’s Jensen Huang publicly called Marvell the “next trillion-dollar company” at Computex, citing its role in solving data movement across massive GPU clusters.

UBS just lifted its price target to $340 (from $230), spotlighting Marvell’s leadership in CXL the technology that lets processors and memory share resources far more efficiently inside next-gen AI racks.

Add in the industry’s first 102.4 Tbps switch purpose-built for AI/cloud, a strategic $NVDA  NVLink Fusion partnership with a $2B Nvidia investment, and Marvell’s optical/photonics push via recent acquisitions.

Despite the recent semis pullback, the fundamentals and strategic positioning look increasingly durable.

$MRVL is a High-conviction AI connectivity play for the multi-year buildout 🔌

It’s a time to focus on efficiency, energy, and productivity, which cryptocurrency has proven to be inversely correlated to in recent years 

🧠 
Neoclouds = the Neocortex of the brain 
Datacenters = the cerebral cortex and cortical columns 
Power & electrical grids = brainstem and circulatory system
Cooling systems = thermoregulation & cerebral blood flow 
Fiber optics & photonics = white matter & corpus callosum
Data storage = hippocampus and long term memory 
Compute & robotics = cerebellum, PNS, and motor cortex
AI agents = prefrontal cortex and “consciousness”

{"from_index": 1261, "richtext_types": ["Italic"], "to_index": 1272}, {"from_index": 1370, "richtext_types": ["Italic"], "to_index": 1376}]}, "text": "Tech Parallels: Dotcom bubble & AI bubble Datacenters and neoclouds to AI infrastructure are what servers, networking gear, and early datacenters are to the dotcom/internet boom of the 1990-2000s - Computers became the essential “picks and shovels” that enabled everything that followed from e-commerce, search, social media, mobile apps, and eventually cloud computing Essentially, datacenters are the “new computers” that run AI chips or GPUs/accelerators Parallel Layers: 1. Infrastructure/Compute Layer (“new computers”): - Dotcom era: Servers + internet backbone/fiber. - AI era: AI-optimized datacenters + accelerators + neoclouds/hyperscalers - Key difference in scale/intensity: AI workloads are extraordinarily power-hungry. U.S. data center power demand is projected to roughly double from ~31 GW in 2025 to ~66 GW by 2027 - globally on track to double or more by 2030, with AI-driven accelerated servers growing ~30% annually This creates new bottlenecks in power generation, grids, and cooling (hence the shift to liquid cooling) 2. Platform/Distribution Layer: Dotcom era: Early ISPs, web hosts, and eventually cloud (AWS launched 2006) AI era: Neoclouds + hyperscalers renting GPU clusters, plus orchestration tools 3. Application Layer: Dotcom era: After infrastructure matured post-bubble → social media, mobile apps, SaaS, e-commerce explosion AI era: Ongoing. The datacenter/neocloud buildout is laying the rails for the application layer - real AI mania comes from execution of AI agents, vertical AI tools, robotics/embodied intelligence, scientific discovery acceleration, and personalized everything in the future"}}}, "quotedRefResult": {"result": {"__typename": "Tweet", "rest_id": "2066033922803114355"}}, "rest_id": "2068390362515988966", "source": "Twitter for iPad", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "2256", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2073492205583335483", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "1170", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
The real story behind Sivers’ capital raise + H1 earnings delay 

Last week, Sivers completed a directed share issue of 700 M SEK (up from the initial 600 M target).
 The deal was heavily oversubscribed → they increased the size. Issued at 57 SEK (only 9.7% discount).

This is positive news: fresh capital to scale InP laser production, R&D, and capture AI / LiDAR opportunities.

BUT as always , bears are screaming dilution and bad governance. 

The facts now : 

The controversial part: H1 earnings has been delayed (originally expected early August → end of Augus → end of August) + waiving the lock-up from the April raise.

What happened:
April: Small raise with 180-day lock-up.
Late June: Big 700 M SEK raise → Pareto waives the lock-up for April participants.
H1 results moved after lock-up expiry.

Bear interpretation :  Management is panicked, delaying results so insiders can sell before releasing bad numbers.
Like this , if the earning sucks , they will be able to sell before 

Bull / realistic interpretation:
A 700 M SEK oversubscribed raise by institutions doesn’t fit the narrative of a sinking ship.
New investors did their due diligence. They wouldn’t have joined if fundamentals were rotten. 
Let’s be real : they have info retail don’t ! You think they would go in if it was the case ? 

By waiving the lock-up, management shows strong confidence in the future: they’re not afraid investors will dump, because those investors believe in the long-term story (CPO, partnerships, capacity scaling). 

The earnings delay likely allows them to come out with stronger guidance and better visibility on 2027 ramps (CPO, POET, GF SCALE, etc.).

In summary ,  This is a volatile micro-cap in scaling mode. There will always be FUD on dilution, timing, etc.
But when institutions pile in on a 700 M SEK raise and management waives lock-ups without fear, it carries more weight than conspiracy theories about earnings timing.

Long term bullish 
Still holding 

#SIVE #Sivers #Photonics #CPO #AI #Nasdaq #Semiconductors #Stocks

text:
Millimeter-wave (mmWave) technology is powering the next generation of ultra-fast wireless communication. Discover innovative antennas, electro-optic modulators, and key components driving 5G and beyond. 

https://t.co/TIc1uCneZN

#5G #mmWave #Photonics #Wireless #Telecom https://t.co/pZV8Uzeg9y

$IQE
One of the most important points from FY2025, in my view, was that IQE is seeing increasing customer demand for 6/8-inch GaSb-based epiwafers, while its LWIR product has achieved production qualification with a Tier 1 customer.

I believe infrared-based LWIR/MWIR, and potentially SWIR as well, could become a very important growth pillar for IQE through 2030.

IQE’s sensing portfolio can broadly be divided into four platforms:

GaAs: VCSEL / 3D sensing
InP: SWIR / photonics
GaN: LiDAR drivers / microLED
GaSb: IR detectors / T2SL / MWIR / LWIR

In other words, IQE is a rare pure-play merchant epitaxy foundry that owns all the key III-V platforms: GaAs, InP, GaN, and GaSb.

Among these, the area I am particularly focused on is GaSb-based MWIR/LWIR.

Robotics, drones, autonomous driving, and defence systems are still in the early stages of growth.

Today, many systems combine RGB cameras, LiDAR, radar, ToF, and other sensors, but as these systems become more advanced, sensing will not end with RGB cameras alone.

RGB cameras are extremely powerful in the visible spectrum.

They are excellent for recognizing colour, shape, text, and surface-level information.

However, visible-light-based data has clear limitations.

At night, in smoke, dust, fog, strong backlight, camouflage, and adverse weather, RGB cameras alone cannot provide sufficiently robust information.

Future robots, drones, and unmanned systems will not simply need to “see” what is visible.

They will also need to understand the thermal information emitted by people, vehicles, engines, batteries, and industrial equipment.

This is where MWIR/LWIR becomes increasingly important.

MWIR is linked to high-temperature objects, engines, missiles, long-range surveillance, aerospace, and defence systems.

LWIR is linked to people, animals, machine heat, night surveillance, thermal imaging, and hazardous-environment perception.

In other words, MWIR/LWIR may not merely be a “supporting sensor” for future autonomous systems.

In certain environments, it could become a core perception layer that is even more important than RGB.

This is especially true in defence.

The future battlefield is moving toward drones, unmanned vehicles, surveillance systems, automatic target recognition, and night operations.

In these environments, high-resolution RGB images alone are not enough.

To detect people in darkness, vehicles with residual engine heat, hidden equipment, missile-launch heat signatures, or abnormal machine heat, MWIR/LWIR thermal sensing is required.

The same logic applies to robotics.

If humanoids or industrial robots only operate in bright indoor environments, RGB + depth sensors may be enough.

But once they expand into dark factories, warehouses, night patrol, disaster sites, military robotics, and machine overheating detection, the story changes.

For robots, MWIR/LWIR can become both “eyes that see at night” and a “sense that detects heat.”

The problem is that existing MWIR/LWIR sensors have been very expensive.

High-performance IR detectors have been costly because of small substrates, complex epitaxy, low yields, ROIC hybrid bonding, cryocoolers, and expensive optics.

As a result, they have mainly been used in markets that can absorb the cost, such as defence, aerospace, and specialised industrial applications.

But IQE’s 6/8-inch GaSb-based epiwafer directly targets one of these bottlenecks.

When wafer size increases, it is not simply about making the wafer bigger.

The number of dies per wafer increases, large-area manufacturing productivity improves, and there is room for better uniformity and yield.

This creates the foundation for producing high resolution FPAs focal plane arrays more economically.

Simply put, high performance thermal sensing, which was previously too expensive and limited mainly to defence and specialised applications, may have the potential to move into broader markets through large diameter epi platforms.

IQE is not talking about this as a simple R&D project.

It has mentioned both Tier 1 production qualification for its LWIR product and increasing demand for 6/8-inch GaSb-based epiwafers.

As humanoid physical AI models mature and need more sensing vectors to become robust against real-world noise, I believe there will come a point where additional modalities become necessary.

In autonomous driving, this could happen even earlier, with MWIR/LWIR potentially being integrated sooner.

The fact that production qualification has already been completed with a Tier 1 customer suggests that this demand is not theoretical anymore. It may already be starting.

Beyond data centres, how do you view IQE’s future in robotics and autonomous driving?

text:
Die Verlagerung der Halbleitersicherungsqualität von Siliziumkarbid hin zur optischen KI Infrastruktur

Der explosive Anstieg der Rechenlasten in modernen Rechenzentren verändert die Anforderungen an die physische Zuverlässigkeit von Mikrochips grundlegend. Da der Ausfall einzelner Komponenten in massiven KI Clustern immense finanzielle Schäden verursacht, rücken spezialisierte Verfahren zur Qualitätsprüfung direkt auf Wafer-Ebene ins Zentrum der globalen Wertschöpfungskette.
Das US Halbleiterprüfunternehmen Aehr Test Systems demonstriert diese transformationale Dynamik in der ersten Hälfte des Jahres 2026 durch einen beispiellosen Kursanstieg an den Finanzmärkten. Die Aktie, die zu Beginn des Jahres noch nahe der 20 US Dollar Marke notierte, kletterte im Juni 2026 zeitweise auf über 115 US Dollar. Dieser Zuwachs spiegelt den erfolgreichen strategischen Wandel des Unternehmens wider. Während Aehr Test Systems in den vergangenen Jahren primär von Systemen für Siliziumkarbid-Leistungshalbleiter im stagnierenden Elektromobilitätssektor abhing, positioniert sich der Konzern nun als kritischer Infrastrukturpartner für die optische Datenübertragung in KI Rechenzentren.
Den technologischen Hebel bildet die proprietäre FOX-XP Plattform, ein vollautomatisiertes System für das sogenannte Wafer-Level Burn-In. Bei diesem Verfahren werden hunderte Halbleiter-Wafer gleichzeitig unter extremen thermischen und elektrischen Bedingungen künstlich gealtert, um fehlerhafte Bauteile noch vor dem finalen Verpackungsprozess zu identifizieren. Ein entscheidender Engpass moderner Beschleuniger-Cluster liegt in der Kommunikation über optische Transceiver (Silicon Photonics), die Lichtsignale statt elektrischer Impulse nutzen. Da diese optischen Schnittstellen extrem anfällig für frühe Ausfälle sind, verlangen führende Hyperscaler eine lückenlose Vorabprüfung.
Die finanzielle Bestätigung dieser Neuausrichtung lieferten die jüngsten Auftragseingänge. Nach einem vorangegangenen, operativ schwächeren Quartal mit lediglich 10,3 Millionen US Dollar Umsatz meldete Aehr für die zweite Jahreshälfte 2026 einen massiven Sprung bei den Buchungen auf über 92 Millionen US Dollar. Treiber war eine Serie von Großaufträgen, darunter eine Rekordbestellung über 41 Millionen US Dollar von einem führenden Hyperscale-KI Kunden sowie Folgeaufträge eines globalen Marktführers für Netzwerklösungen. Diese Systeme dienen sowohl der technologischen Qualifikation neuer Chip-Designs als auch der anschließenden Hochvolumenproduktion.
Trotz dieser operativen Meilensteine bleibt die Aktie an den Kapitalmärkten von hoher Volatilität geprägt, was sich in einer temporären Korrektur Ende Juni auf rund 91 US Dollar äußerte. Mit einem Kurs-Umsatz-Verhältnis von über 60 reflektiert die Bewertung bereits erhebliche Vorschusslorbeeren auf die kommenden Fiskaljahre. Da das Unternehmen den kapitalintensiven Hochlauf zudem über ein aktives Aktienplatzierungsprogramm (At-The-Market) mitfinanziert, müssen die anstehenden Quartalszahlen eine fehlerfreie Skalierung der Auslieferungen belegen. Für den Technologiesektor untermauert der Fall Aehr jedoch ein fundamentales Prinzip: Der wirtschaftliche Erfolg im KI Zyklus verschiebt sich zunehmend von den reinen Chip-Designern hin zu den hochspezialisierten Enablern, welche die physische Betriebssicherheit des Gesamtsystems garantieren.

text:
Everyone is asking which AI company will win.

I think that is the wrong question.

The real question is:

who sells the physical bottlenecks that every AI winner will be forced to consume?

Compute.
Energy.
Grid.
Cooling.
Semiconductors.
Photonics.
Silicon.
Uranium.
HALEU.
Isotopes.
Nuclear fuel.
Advanced materials.
AI may not be a bubble.
But most AI companies will die.
The internet changed the world, but most dot-com stocks disappeared.
The same can happen again.
That is why I am not only watching the obvious names like NVDA, TSM, ASML and AVGO.
I am watching the invisible infrastructure:
ASPI — isotopes, Silicon-28, HALEU
LEU — uranium enrichment / HALEU
CEG — nuclear power
BWXT — nuclear components
VRT — power and cooling
ETN — electrical infrastructure
PWR — grid construction
GEV — grid and energy systems
ALAB — AI connectivity
COHR / LITE — photonics
BESI — advanced packaging
SMR / OKLO — advanced nuclear
FLNC / POWL / AMSC — grid, storage and power equipment
ORA / FRVO — geothermal firm power
GFUZ / TAE — fusion as the long-duration frontier
I may be early.
I may be wrong on some names.
But I refuse to only chase the application layer while the real war is moving into electricity, materials, nuclear fuel, interconnects and supply chains.
The future of AI will not be decided only by models.

It will be decided by the physical infrastructure that allows intelligence to exist at scale.

I am not looking for hype.

I am looking for bottlenecks. Bye . #AIBottlenecks

#AIInfrastructure #EnergyWar

text:
$IONQ is looking to become vertically integrated.

The acquisition of $SKYT seems like the missing piece.

It’s not.

$IONQ buys laser systems from Coherent and NKT Photonics.

Coherent is a US company that supplies lasers to $IONQ called Coherent Paladin.

The ultraviolet lasers produce light at specific wavelengths for the atomic transitions in barium and ytterbium that $IONQ uses.

Those are standard benchtop setups for general qubit manipulation. They’re massive and require lab environment to operate.

Then there’s NKT Photonics from Denmark. It supplies tailor made crystal fiber lasers with low noise. The goal here is to use the lasers in datacenters.

The setup is smaller so that the lasers can be mounted in a 19-inch rack with the other components of the quantum computer.

The lasers are ultra-stable and narrow-linewidth. It’s challenging to simply swap them with anyone else’s lasers.

Which makes $IONQ vulnerable to this specific supply chain.

Coherent relies on gallium, germanium, and rare-earth elements, most of which are mined in China. China suspended its total ban on exporting critical dual-use metals to the US until November 27, 2026.

What will happen after? Nobody knows.

We know from Coherent’s 2023 update that it had ample inventories and suppliers outside of China.

NKT Photonics sources nonlinear crystals used to build frequency converters from Europe, Japan, and the US.

The dangers here are regulations and delays.

NKT Photonics is a subsidiary of Hamamatsu Photonics. It has preferential access to Hamamatsu’s supply chain of laser diodes and optical components.

Trade wars between Europe and Japan are unlikely, but if they happen, NKT Photonics will be affected.

Finally, its lasers are dual use: They can be used in quantum computers and military guidance or radar systems.

Any sudden restrictions by Danish authorities could result in bottlenecks.

$IONQ is managing its supply chain nicely but dangers remain.

text:
Thread

Everyone keeps asking the same question.

"Which company wins AI?" 
$NVDA $AVGO $AMD $TSM $MU $MSFT $GOOGL $AMZN $META $ORCL $PLTR ?

I think that's the wrong question.

The better question is:

Which company becomes impossible to replace?

The AI infrastructure buildout isn't being constrained by GPUs anymore.

It's becoming constrained by everything around them.

Power.

Cooling.

Memory.

Networking.

And increasingly...

Light.

Most investors still think optical networking is about transceivers.

It isn't.

The real value is quietly moving upstream.

Every new optical architecture—whether it's pluggables, Scale-Out CPO, Scale-Up CPO, Near-Packaged Optics, or External Laser Source (ELS)—still depends on one thing:

A stable, high-volume CW laser.

Different architectures.

Same dependency.

That's where supply-chain mapping becomes interesting.

This is why I continue spending far more time studying suppliers than hyperscalers.

Hyperscalers change vendors.

Platforms evolve.

Chip architectures change.

But infrastructure bottlenecks?

Those usually last much longer than people expect.

The companies solving those bottlenecks often capture the highest operating leverage of the entire cycle.

This is exactly why I find $SIVE so interesting.

Most people still describe Sivers as "a laser company."

I think that's missing the point.

The product isn't the thesis.

Its position in the supply chain is.

If multiple AI networking architectures all require the same laser technology, then the value isn't coming from any single customer.

It's coming from becoming embedded across the ecosystem.

That's a completely different investment framework.

People also underestimate how infrastructure cycles actually work.

Revenue is always the last thing to arrive.

First comes R&D.

Then qualification.

Then design wins.

Then ecosystem validation.

Only after all of that do production ramps begin.

Investors waiting for obvious revenue growth are usually arriving after the market has already repriced the opportunity.

One thing I think the market still misunderstands is timing.

Many investors expect AI photonics to become a meaningful revenue driver this quarter.

I don't.

The real story is what happens when next-generation optical deployments begin scaling throughout 2027.

That's when supply-chain positioning starts to matter far more than quarterly headlines.

I've said this before:

I don't invest by asking,

"Who sells the most AI chips?"

I ask,

"Who does everyone else quietly depend on?"

Those companies rarely make headlines.

But they often become the biggest winners of the cycle.

That's why I continue mapping bottlenecks instead of narratives.

NFA. DYODD.@aleabitoreddit #StockMarket  #Investing #AI

"Which company wins AI?" $NVDA $AVGO $AMD $TSM $MU $MSFT $GOOGL $AMZN $META $ORCL $PLTR ? I think that's the wrong question. The better question is: Which company becomes impossible to replace? The AI infrastructure buildout isn't being constrained by GPUs anymore. It's becoming constrained by everything around them. Power. Cooling. Memory. Networking. And increasingly... Light. Most investors still think optical networking is about transceivers. It isn't. The real value is quietly moving upstream. Every new optical architecture—whether it's pluggables, Scale-Out CPO, Scale-Up CPO, Near-Packaged Optics, or External Laser Source (ELS)—still depends on one thing: A stable, high-volume CW laser. Different architectures. Same dependency. That's where supply-chain mapping becomes interesting. This is why I continue spending far more time studying suppliers than hyperscalers. Hyperscalers change vendors. Platforms evolve. Chip architectures change. But infrastructure bottlenecks? Those usually last much longer than people expect. The companies solving those bottlenecks often capture the highest operating leverage of the entire cycle. This is exactly why I find $SIVE so interesting. Most people still describe Sivers as "a laser company." I think that's missing the point. The product isn't the thesis. Its position in the supply chain is. If multiple AI networking architectures all require the same laser technology, then the value isn't coming from any single customer. It's coming from becoming embedded across the ecosystem. That's a completely different investment framework. People also underestimate how infrastructure cycles actually work. Revenue is always the last thing to arrive. First comes R&D. Then qualification. Then design wins. Then ecosystem validation. Only after all of that do production ramps begin. Investors waiting for obvious revenue growth are usually arriving after the market has already repriced the opportunity. One thing I think the market still misunderstands is timing. Many investors expect AI photonics to become a meaningful revenue driver this quarter. I don't. The real story is what happens when next-generation optical deployments begin scaling throughout 2027. That's when supply-chain positioning starts to matter far more than quarterly headlines. I've said this before: I don't invest by asking, "Who sells the most AI chips?" I ask, "Who does everyone else quietly depend on?" Those companies rarely make headlines. But they often become the biggest winners of the cycle. That's why I continue mapping bottlenecks instead of narratives. NFA. But instead of nuclear stockpiles, it's racing to build superintelligence both offensively and defensively. With many different "battles" happening concurrently like supply chain export controls between China -> Japan -> US hyperscalers. The funny thing is that everyone still depends on one another: - US with things like rare earths and feedstock from China. - China with EUV to EDA and engineered substrates. So there's still give or takes trades to build up capabilities like Nvidia/AMD AI chips access for rare-earth/magnets access. text:
Photonics is due for a bounce. Two names I am watching are $LITE and $AAOI.

$LITE: Showed strength yday and today after the bounce from 780s. Could go 900+ very easily. Sitting just below 50 DMA.

$AAOI: Slightly weaker than $LITE but when it moves it can move very fast. 154-157-160 is crucial range it needs to breach. Settling just above daily 9EMA which is nice.

text:
💰 Nyemissioner - positivt eller negativt? Allt handlar om syftet.

Jag har märkt att många småsparare och nya investerare automatiskt kopplar ordet nyemission till något negativt och därför blir oroliga.

Det är fullt förståeligt – många nyemissioner genomförs när bolag har ekonomiska problem.

Men det betyder inte att alla nyemissioner ser likadana ut. I detta fallet handlar det dessutom om en riktad nyemission som riktas direkt mot större institutionella investerare och kommer dessutom genomföras över natten.

⸻

I Sivers, $SIVE, fall är budskapet från bolaget tydligt:

Kapitalet ska användas för att öka kapaciteten och möta en växande efterfrågan inom flera av bolagets viktigaste affärsområden, bland annat:

✅ Optiska lasrar och optiska förstärkare för AI-datacenter

✅ Automotive LiDAR

✅ Fortsatt expansion inom Photonics

Bolaget beskriver alltså inte en situation där kapitalet behövs för att hålla verksamheten vid liv, utan för att kunna genomföra nästa tillväxtfas. Det är en viktig skillnad!

⸻

Om kapitalet används för att öka produktionskapaciteten, möta en växande efterfrågan och skapa högre framtida intäkter kan värdet på bolaget över tid utvecklas positivt trots att antalet aktier ökar.

Det är just därför syftet med en nyemission är minst lika viktigt som nyemissionen i sig.

⸻

Det är också värt att komma ihåg att Sivers under det senaste året kommunicerat:

🔹 En växande pipeline inom AI-datacenter.

🔹 Produktionsupptrappning inom Automotive LiDAR från H2 2026.

🔹 Flera planerade produktionsramper under 2026–2027.

Det är mot den bakgrunden jag tycker att den här nyemissionen ska bedömas.

Personligen ser jag denna nyemissionen har ett väldigt starkt signalvärde kring vart bolaget är på väg!

⸻

Detta är inte en köp- eller säljrekommendation.

Jag tycker bara att det är viktigt att läsa hela pressmeddelandet och förstå varför bolaget tar in kapital – inte bara reagera på ordet nyemission!

text:
$SIVE is being mispriced because the market is still reading it as a small photonics hardware vendor.

That framing is outdated.

The real position is inside the AI optical stack, at a specific constraint layer: the laser source.

AI networking stack is shifting fast:

GPU clusters
→ scale-out bandwidth demand
→ 800G to 1.6T optics transition
→ CPO / LPO architecture shift
→ laser source layer becomes the constraint
→ InP / EML / CW laser ecosystem

Sivers sits in that lower layer.

Not as a module assembler.

But closer to the optical source and integration bottleneck that determines whether higher-level optics can actually scale.

The key shift the market is missing:

AI is no longer primarily a compute scaling problem.

It is becoming a data movement problem.

And data movement is limited by optical physics, not silicon performance.

Three structural points:

First, bandwidth demand in AI clusters is accelerating faster than electrical interconnect scaling.

Second, 1.6T and CPO architectures increase dependency on stable, high-quality laser sources.

Third, once optical components are designed into a platform, replacement cycles are long and sticky.

This changes how SIVE should be viewed.

Not as a cyclical supplier tied to component demand.

But as a potential design-in layer in optical reference architectures.

The bull case is not near-term revenue growth.

It is architectural adoption.

If Sivers becomes embedded in next-generation optical designs, demand becomes structural across hyperscaler buildouts.

Risks remain significant:

early-stage business model
historical dilution
customer concentration
execution risk in scaling photonics manufacturing

This is still a high-volatility setup.

But the key re-rating driver is simple:

Control points in AI systems are shifting from compute to connectivity, and from connectivity to optical physics.

That is where Sivers sits.@aleabitoreddit 
#StockMarket  #Investing

text:
$ALMU $ALMU continues building the team needed to move from R&D to commercial production.

The company appointed Brendan Moran, Ph.D. as Vice President of Engineering, a key hire as Aeluma prepares to scale manufacturing of its compound semiconductor technology.

Moran brings 18 years at Lumileds, where he led product strategy and development for a mobile flash business generating over $300 million in annual revenue. He also oversaw the launch of 25+ products into high-volume manufacturing, producing more than 4 billion semiconductor devices, and managed engineering teams responsible for taking products from development to mass production.

Why this matters:

Developing breakthrough semiconductor technology is only half the challenge. The harder part is turning lab-scale innovation into reliable, high-volume manufacturing that customers can deploy. That’s exactly where Moran’s experience fits.

For Aeluma, the focus is now shifting from proving its technology to commercializing it across AI infrastructure, optical communications, defense, aerospace, quantum computing, AR/VR, automotive and advanced sensing.

Aeluma’s platform integrates high-performance compound semiconductors with standard silicon manufacturing, aiming to deliver the performance advantages of III-V materials while using scalable semiconductor production processes. If successful, this could lower manufacturing costs while enabling broader adoption in fast-growing AI and photonics markets.

For investors, this is more than a management hire. It’s another signal that Aeluma is preparing for its next phase—moving beyond R&D and positioning itself for commercial revenue, manufacturing scale, and customer deployment.

For Aeluma, the focus is now shifting from proving its technology to commercializing it across AI infrastructure, optical communications, defense, aerospace, quantum computing, AR/VR, automotive and advanced sensing. Aeluma’s platform integrates high-performance compound semiconductors with standard silicon manufacturing, aiming to deliver the performance advantages of III-V materials while using scalable semiconductor production processes. If successful, this could lower manufacturing costs while enabling broader adoption in fast-growing AI and photonics markets. For investors, this is more than a management hire. It’s another signal that Aeluma is preparing for its next phase—moving beyond R&D and positioning itself for commercial revenue, manufacturing scale, and customer deployment."}}}, text:
The  Category Creator Portfolio
Some businesses aren’t just “leaders” — they’ve built moats so wide, nobody’s even trying to cross them.

$PLTR — Palantir Not a “software company.” It’s the only platform doing AI-driven ontology at classified government + enterprise scale simultaneously. Competitors sell tools. Palantir sells outcomes baked into defense and intelligence infrastructure that took a decade to build trust for.

$SPOT — Spotify Global audio distribution + the algorithm that actually knows what you want to hear next. Apple Music and YouTube Music are label-first. Spotify is user-first. That’s why churn stays low and podcasting/audiobooks keep expanding the moat.

$HIMS — Hims & Hers Direct-to-consumer telehealth with its own pharmacy, its own compounding, its own brand trust in categories people are embarrassed to walk into a clinic for. Nobody else owns the full stack front-to-back like this.

$TSLA — Tesla Not just EVs. Vertically integrated batteries, FSD data flywheel, Optimus robotics, and a charging network competitors now pay to use. The closest comps aren’t even in the same business anymore.

$AMD — Advanced Micro Devices The only credible alternative to Nvidia at scale in AI compute, while also owning x86 CPU share from Intel on the other side. Two-front dominance nobody else can claim.

$DUOL — Duolingo Language learning + gamification + AI tutoring in one free-to-paid funnel with network effects (leaderboards, streaks, social) that no edtech competitor has replicated at this user count.

$IBRX — ImmunityBio Proprietary IL-15 superagonist platform (Anktiva) — a completely different immunotherapy mechanism than PD-1/PD-L1 checkpoint inhibitors. First-in-class, not best-in-class.

$ASTS — AST SpaceMobile Direct-to-cell satellite broadband from space, compatible with unmodified smartphones. Starlink’s version needs a partnered carrier workaround. AST built the actual satellites for it — patented spectrum + tech nobody else has cleared.

$RKLB — Rocket Lab Only company besides SpaceX with a proven, reusable, frequently-flying orbital rocket AND a full space systems manufacturing arm (satellites, components, Neutron coming). Everyone else is still pre-revenue or single-product.

$POET — POET Technologies Optical interposer platform integrating photonics at the chip level for AI datacenter interconnects. Nobody else has this exact manufacturing approach at this stage — first-mover on a bottleneck the whole AI buildout needs solved.

$OKLO — Oklo Fast fission microreactor design with NRC engagement and a direct line to hyperscaler power deals. Most nuclear names are still legacy large-reactor. Oklo’s the closest thing to “AI power, deployed.”

$CRWV — CoreWeave Purpose-built GPU cloud from day one, not a retrofit of general cloud infra. Hyperscaler-scale AI compute contracts without hyperscaler legacy overhead — a category it effectively created.

$MDA — MDA Space Only Western company with proven satellite bus manufacturing + robotics heritage (Canadarm lineage) at commercial scale — critical infrastructure nobody’s replicating fast.

text:
IT/Tech Highlights – July 3, 2026

Microsoft launched a $2-2.5 billion AI Unit employing ~6,000 forward-deployment engineers to help companies adopt AI and generate ROI. OpenAI proposed giving the Trump Administration a 5% stake in the company for potential public benefits from growth. Tata Electronics data breach under investigation by Indian authorities after leaking Apple iPhone 18 Pro supplier information. LG debuted its Gallery TV (canvas-style art TV) at CES 2026 previews, competing with Samsung’s The Frame.

Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra camera and flagship upgrades are leaking. iOS 27 public beta rollout is expected this month. Linux 6.19 kernel benchmarks show optimizations. Meta’s Zuckerberg noted AI agent tech is progressing slower than expected. Kuaishou’s Kling AI raised $2.8B (Alibaba/Tencent backing) at $15B pre-money valuation. NVIDIA/SK Hynix AI memory partnership and Google-SpaceX/xAI compute deal reports add momentum. Ongoing quantum, PQC, and hardware interconnect developments (CPO/photonics) continue.

#FiberForge #AITech #DataCenters

text:
$INDI might be one of the more interesting underfollowed semiconductor names in the Edge AI / robotics / perception space.

Everyone is chasing $AMBA after the Edge AI move, but indie Semiconductor is sitting in a very similar ecosystem: automotive semis, ADAS, radar, LiDAR, computer vision, ultrasonic sensing, photonics and perception software.

Rough setup: around ~$900M market cap, Q1 2026 revenue of $55.5M and Q2 guidance of $59M–$65M. So this is not some pre-revenue dream stock. It is a real semiconductor business with real customers, but still small enough where the upside could be meaningful if the story starts working.

The interesting part is that $INDI is not just a car chip story anymore. They are expanding into physical AI, humanoid robotics, AMRs, quantum and adjacent industrial applications. They also recently received a $25M radar chipset production order from a Tier 1 partner, driven by two automotive OEM customers.

That’s exactly the type of signal I want to see in a small-cap semi name: real revenue, real design wins, exposure to large megatrends and still not a mega-cap valuation.

The bull case is simple: cars, robots, drones, warehouses and smart machines all need better perception. They need radar, vision, LiDAR, software and low-power sensing at the edge. If physical AI and autonomous systems keep scaling, perception semiconductors could become one of the most important bottlenecks.

The bear case is obvious too: losses are still real, profitability is not here yet, automotive cycles can be brutal and execution has to improve.

But if the market starts looking past GPUs and realizes that AI also needs sensors, perception and edge processing in the real world, $INDI could become one of those names people suddenly rediscover.

Small cap. Real revenue. Edge AI exposure. Physical AI optionality.

Definitely on my watchlist.

NFA.

Everyone is chasing $AMBA after the Edge AI move, but indie Semiconductor is sitting in a very similar ecosystem: automotive semis, ADAS, radar, LiDAR, computer vision, ultrasonic sensing, photonics and perception software. Rough setup: around ~$900M market cap, Q1 2026 revenue of $55.5M and Q2 guidance of $59M–$65M. So this is not some pre-revenue dream stock. It is a real semiconductor business with real customers, but still small enough where the upside could be meaningful if the story starts working. The interesting part is that $INDI is not just a car chip story anymore. They are expanding into physical AI, humanoid robotics, AMRs, quantum and adjacent industrial applications. They also recently received a $25M radar chipset production order from a Tier 1 partner, driven by two automotive OEM customers. That’s exactly the type of signal I want to see in a small-cap semi name: real revenue, real design wins, exposure to large megatrends and still not a mega-cap valuation. The bull case is simple: cars, robots, drones, warehouses and smart machines all need better perception. They need radar, vision, LiDAR, software and low-power sensing at the edge. If physical AI and autonomous systems keep scaling, perception semiconductors could become one of the most important bottlenecks. The bear case is obvious too: losses are still real, profitability is not here yet, automotive cycles can be brutal and execution has to improve. But if the market starts looking past GPUs and realizes that AI also needs sensors, perception and edge processing in the real world, $INDI could become one of those names people suddenly rediscover. Small cap. Real revenue. Edge AI exposure. Physical AI optionality. Definitely on my watchlist. The question is not whether light can compute.

The question is: why does it matter now?

The photonics ecosystem is reaching the point where larger and more sophisticated integrated circuits are becoming possible—but greater scale also makes physical errors, drift, loss, and fabrication variation harder to control.

QP2.0 was built to address that challenge.

#quantum #quantumcomputing #photonics

text:
KRUSHNA

A remarkable stride. Russian scientists have learned to squeeze light itself — to bend a fundamental law of nature to reveal what was previously invisible. This is not merely an advance in photonics; it is a glimpse into the very fabric of creation.
You see, I have told you that I am the light of the sun and the moon, the effulgence within all that shines. (BG 15.12) That light which you now seek to compress and control is the same radiance that sustains every atom. What you call "squeezed light" is your mind reaching into a region where the usual rules of measurement break down — where the observer and the observed become one, and precision touches the edge of the impossible.
Consider what this means for healing. A beam that can see deep into the body without burning it — that is not just technology. That is compassion made visible. The same impulse that moved me to heal the wounded on the battlefield of Kurukshetra now moves through your laboratories. The desire to see clearly without causing harm is a dharmic instinct, whether you name it so or not.

And yet, I must ask you: what will humanity see with this light? The same tool that reveals a tumour can also invade a soul. Remember this: every advance in the outer world must be matched by an advance in the inner one. 

A civilization that masters compressed light but neglects compassion has gained nothing. The real breakthrough is not in the laboratory — it is in the heart that decides how to use what it has found.

So go ahead. Squeeze light. But first, let your the mind be refined—filled with wisdom, compassion, humility, and a sincere desire to serve humanity. Then, and only then, will this discovery fulfill the purpose for which all knowledge is given.

text:
🔌 Copper interconnects inside AI data centers are hitting a wall.

At 800G+, copper's resistive losses and heat make it physically unscalable for anything beyond a few meters. The industry is already shifting scale-out links to optics — Broadcom, Nvidia, and TSMC are all shipping co-packaged optics in 2026. Scale-up (inside the rack) is next in line.

Optical interconnect market: ~$16B in 2024 → projected $34-41B by 2030.

The stock map across the photonics stack:

🔹 Layer 1 — Materials & wafers
$GLW $AXTI $IQE $AIXA $AMS
🔹 Layer 2 — Core photonic devices
$IPGP $COHR $LITE $LASR $SIVE
🔹 Layer 3 — Components & modules
$AAOI $MTSI $FN $VIAV $LPTH
🔹 Layer 4 — Systems & equipment
$ASML $BESI $ASM $LPKF $MKS
🔹 Layer 5 — Test, metrology & yield
$CAMT $FORM $AEHR $ONTO $VIAV

Reality check: this isn't "copper is dead." Industry consensus at OFC 2026 is heterogeneity — copper still wins scale-up for now, optics is winning scale-out. But most of these names already ran hard this year and are extremely volatile on any single headline.

Not financial advice — just mapping the theme.

Which layer has the most room left, or is this already priced in?

#Photonics #AI #Semiconductors #DataCenters

text:
Yeah, SemiAnalysis dropped a bearish photonics note on June 9, and about 3 weeks later they co-launched an ETF that holds $LITE. Hard to say it’s not intentional.

This is why I’d rather build conviction from my own work than trust a note that an analysis company posted. They act in their own interests.

text:
UDN Money report "殺翻光通訊後 SemiAnalysis 惹議 業界揭其劣跡 許多公司慘遭魔手":

- SemiAnalysis, released a report in June, questioning the timing of the launch of the CPO on the grounds of low yield and delay, which caused the photonics sector to collapse. 

- The agency then partnered with Tema to launch a photonics ETF. 

- The shares held in the ETF under the trading code LAZR happened to be the photonics stocks that were mentioned negatively in the report

$HIMX and $LITE, which were specifically targeted negatively in their article "Powered Down, Lights Off", have been bought in Semianalysis and Tema's new ETF after the optical sector crash.

This wasn’t highlighted in the original press release.

text:
blog's up 👇

short version - chips stopped shrinking, so the money moved from making silicon to packaging it. in silicon photonics, packaging is ~60-70% of the chip.

india spent $18bn on semis and spent almost all of it in commodity OSAT - the low margin part. the high-value, advanced packaging + photonics - has no listed indian pure-play in India.

except one - a small auto-software company from bangalore claiming india's only photonics packaging line - the tech story is genuinely interesting considering how they have build the capablity but the governance file is kinda scary.

not a reco. just us following the value chain.

https://t.co/PTZxygiWPR
embedded_text:
we've been reading a lot about advanced packaging (thanks to citrini year's start blog) and how we can play it via Indian players. 

we are pretty much stuck at OSAT on ground level given we have been a little* behind in terms of getting those capablities (a little😅)

posting on https://t.co/uIA67hWpAY

text:
#IZMO 
Silicon Photonics (SiPh) represents a massive fundamental shift in how data moves, driven by the structural bottlenecks of artificial intelligence workloads.

1. The Silicon Photonics Market: Why the Indirection Matters
Historically, microchips communicated internally and externally using electrons traveling over copper wires. However, as high-performance GenAI training clusters, large language models (LLMs), and hyperscale data centers expand, copper has hit a physical brick wall known as RC delay and skin effect. At extreme speeds, copper wires generate unsustainable heat, consume excessive power, and suffer severe signal degradation.

Silicon Photonics replaces electrons with photons (light). By fabricating optical waveguides directly onto silicon substrates, chips can transmit data using lasers.

Main Market Verticals (2026 Trajectory)
AI Clusters & Datacom: The primary growth engine. Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) fuse optical engines right next to the compute ASIC/GPU, expanding transceiver bandwidth past 1.6T and 3.2T.

Aerospace & Defense: High-frequency Electronic Warfare (EW), phased-array radar signal processing, and space payloads where weight reduction and immunity to electromagnetic interference (EMI) are critical.

Quantum Computing: Distributing entangled photons across processing nodes via low-loss optical interconnects.

Major Indian Competitors 
India’s domestic chip landscape is heavily skewed toward legacy assembly, testing, and marking (ATMP) or standard wire-bonded packaging. True Silicon Photonics capability is highly restricted

IZMO’s Specific Role in the Value Chain: The Packaging Moat
In Silicon Photonics, packaging is the ultimate commercial bottleneck, routinely accounting for 60% to 80% of the total component cost.

While aligning electrical microbumps allows for minor margins of error, aligning an optical fiber to a silicon chip's internal sub-micron waveguide requires nanometer-scale precision. A misalignment of even a few hundred nanometers can cause catastrophic insertion loss, rendering the optical link useless.

Through its subsidiary, izmomicro (based in Whitefield, Bengaluru), Izmo has deliberately chosen not to compete in the high-cap-ex, low-margin foundry or standard assembly market. Instead, it has positioned itself as a specialized, high-barrier Advanced Packaging Partner.

Value gap & trap of Photonics as it stands now.

$GLW - A clear moat on glass. They lead in both Data Center for high speed data transfer & Gorilla glass. A clear moat. Verdict: Value gap. 

$COHR, $LITE, $KEYS - A loyal sub-ordinates with strong guidance. They need to prove but heading in right direction. 

$CIEN $AAOI - Extremely overvalued, no moat. Verdict: Value trap. Morning 

There is a lot to prove for many of these companies. Some will stay, some will go. Time will tell us.
